message.content:
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN) or
OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries no new particular, MAY be non-entailed). THE VERBATIM
SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE; the model-written `claim` is a display cache and nothing is validated against it.

=== STANDING ORDERS ===
GENERALITY IS NOT OPTIONAL. Every mechanism must state its behaviour on a CLINICAL question, a LEGAL
question, and a THIN-EVIDENCE question (where "the literature does not settle this" is the CORRECT answer
and saying so is a PASS). A domain change must be a DATA edit (a new registry row), never a CODE edit.
THE TELL WE KEEP FAILING: a hand-written regex, a hardcoded topic gate, a domain insight baked into code.

=== WHAT ALREADY EXISTS — BUILD ON IT, DO NOT DUPLICATE IT ===
The integrity layer is BUILT AND WIRED (another workflow just finished it):
  acquisition.py   -- THE ONE DOOR TO THE NETWORK. It emits observations, never statuses. A 429 lands as
                      THROTTLED; the reducer derives BACKEND_FAILED; nothing can turn it into "no evidence".
                      USE Acquirer.get_json / record_manifestation. Do NOT open your own urllib path.
  provenance.py    -- Work / Expression / Manifestation / typed edges. bind_span, resolve_attribution,
                      verify_span. Spans bind to manifestation_id + content_hash, never to a DOI.
  event_ledger.py  -- pure reducers over an append-only ledger. Content completeness is per-artifact-kind
                      (a judicial opinion is complete at 105 words; a journal article is not).
  research_contract.py -- compiles a question -> contract -> coverage matrix -> outline -> facets.
                      coverage cell status: UNSEARCHED|SEARCH_FAILED|UNROUTED|SEARCHED_NONE|THIN|SUPPORTED|
                      CONFLICTED. Only SEARCHED_NONE after route completion licenses an absence statement.
  evidence_miner.py -- typed evidence acts (config/evidence_acts.json); binds at card construction.
Read these first. Everything you build plugs into them.


=== SOL PLAN 4 — THE WINNING CORPUS. THIS IS THE DESIGN. EXECUTE IT, DO NOT REDESIGN IT. ===

The corpus is TEN journal-attributable works (we claimed 32). cellcog has ~98 sources. But bodhi scores
0.5441 with ONE venue mention -- so the objective is NOT "100 papers" or "202 numbers". It is ENOUGH
INDEPENDENT, INTERPRETABLE EVIDENCE to close the question's important cells and create high-value contrasts.

Sol's forecast: SOTA-class corpus = +0.035 to +0.065; combined with synthesis architecture, 0.520-0.580.
cellcog's 0.5603 is inside that band.


YOUR TASK — Sol plan 4 item 2: OA-NATIVE DISCOVERY ROUTES + THE CAPABILITY-BASED SOURCE ROUTER.
Build /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/source_router.py.

The strategy must change from "choose canonical paywalled works, then chase copies" (which gave a 19%
hit rate) to "OA-FIRST DISCOVERY plus version pursuit." Build a DECLARATIVE, DATA-DRIVEN router:

  config/source_routes.yaml (or .json) -- ONE ROW PER ROUTE, keyed by EVIDENCE CAPABILITY not domain:
     adapter_id | evidence_roles | document_types | jurisdiction_coverage | query_dialects |
     identifier_types | version_resolvers | rate_policy | coverage_note

  Representative rows (Sol named these): DOAJ (13M+ article records), PMC + Europe PMC (OA full-text XML),
  OpenAlex + Unpaywall (enumerate locations -- never treat their version label as final), CORE
  (institutional repositories), OpenAIRE, Zenodo/HAL, Crossref (identity/dates/relations, NOT full text),
  NBER/IZA/RePEc/SSRN/arXiv/medRxiv (separate-work + preprint discovery, admissible only when policy
  permits that expression), official legal systems (GovInfo, EUR-Lex, CourtListener).

  The router performs SET COVER over the plan's required evidence roles and invokes every matching route
  family. IT DOES NOT ask an LLM to remember that economists use NBER or clinicians use PubMed -- the
  routing is DERIVED from the contract's evidence roles against the route table.

  ALL discovery goes through acquisition.Acquirer -- so every attempt is on the ledger, and these outcomes
  stay DISTINCT: FETCHED | NOT_FOUND | ACCESS_DENIED | THROTTLED | TRANSIENT_ERROR | LANDING_PAGE |
  ABSTRACT_ONLY | WRONG_WORK | CORRUPT_EXTRACTION. Only NOT_FOUND across exhausted applicable routes
  supports "we did not locate an accessible copy." A 429 NEVER does.

Clinical: routes to PubMed/PMC/Europe PMC/ClinicalTrials.gov. Legal: GovInfo/EUR-Lex/CourtListener/SSRN,
NOT NBER. Adding a domain that existing adapters serve is ONLY NEW ROWS.

Include a __main__ that, for task 72 and for a CLINICAL question and a LEGAL question, PRINTS which routes
fire. If the clinical question routes to NBER, IT IS BROKEN and you must say so. Do NOT do a full live
crawl (slow, and rate-limits); prove the ROUTING is correct with a dry run + a handful of live probes.

Return: what you built, the route table, and the three routing dry-runs.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""ACQUISITION — THE ONLY DOOR TO THE NETWORK, AND IT CANNOT CONCLUDE.
3	
4	    Sol (a)(1): "ACQUISITION EMITS OBSERVATIONS AND MANIFESTATIONS, NEVER STATUSES."
5	
6	THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR
7	
8	  Tonight a forced HTTP 429 on Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) — whose free copy provably exists, NBER
9	  Working Paper 8337, in full, forever — was written to disk as `content_status = CITATION_ONLY`.
10	  CITATION_ONLY is the MINER'S EXCLUSION LABEL. A transient throttle PERMANENTLY DELETED the
11	  most-cited paper in the literature from our evidence base.
12	
13	  A FACT ABOUT OUR REQUEST RATE, WRITTEN TO DISK AS A FACT ABOUT THE WORLD.
14	
15	  The mechanism was three lines long and lived in every fetcher:
16	
17	      d = jget(url)          # returns None on ANY exception — 429, 404, timeout, DNS, bad JSON
18	      if not d:              # ...and the caller reads that None as
19	          return []          # "NO FREE COPY OF THIS PAPER EXISTS"
20	
21	  `None` is four different worlds — THEIR INDEX, OUR REQUEST RATE, THEIR ENTITLEMENT WALL, and a HANG —
22	  and every one of them collapsed into the one word the miner obeys.
23	
24	WHY A GUARD ON THE FETCHERS WOULD NOT HAVE HELPED
25	
26	  It is not that the fetchers concluded WRONGLY. It is that they concluded AT ALL. So this module does
27	  not check their conclusions: IT REMOVES THE VOCABULARY. There is no `content_status=` parameter in
28	  this API. There is no `fulltext_source=`. A fetcher physically cannot say "still paywalled", because
29	  nothing here accepts the sentence. It may say `http_status=429`, and a REDUCER nobody can bypass
30	  turns that into THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED — which is not an absence, and never becomes one.
31	
32	THE EXACT EVENT SEQUENCE, PER REQUESTED WORK (Sol's, verbatim)
33	
34	    1. ROUTE_PLANNED            in each fetcher's main(), before its adapter loop
35	    2. BACKEND_ATTEMPTED        immediately before each network request
36	    3. at the exception boundary, EXACTLY ONE of: RESPONSE_RECEIVED | THROTTLED | BLOCKED
37	    4. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED     when a URL/result is returned
38	    5. MANIFESTATION_FETCHED    after bytes are obtained — locator, immutable blob id, byte hash,
39	                                requested identity, adapter observations
40	    6. CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED  only from the shared artifact-profile reducer
41	
42	  Step 6 takes NO PAYLOAD FROM THE CALLER. `record_manifestation()` computes it by calling
43	  `event_ledger.observe_text()` itself. A fetcher cannot hand-build a profile, so it cannot inflate
44	  one — which is how a 535-word cookie banner came to be stamped FULLTEXT with a word count of 8,000.
45	
46	MANIFESTATIONS ARE IMMUTABLE AND CONTENT-ADDRESSED
47	
48	  The bytes go to a content-addressed blob store, keyed by sha256 OF THE BYTES. Two fetchers that
49	  retrieve the same document write the same blob. Two fetchers that retrieve DIFFERENT documents for
50	  one DOI write TWO blobs, and NEITHER IS DELETED — which is what lets merge_corpus stop holding a
51	  fight between them and picking the longer one.
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import hashlib
56	import io
57	import itertools
58	import json
59	import os
60	import re
61	import sys
62	import time
63	import urllib.error
64	import urllib.parse
65	import urllib.request
66	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
67	from pathlib import Path
68	from typing import Any, Iterable
69	
70	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
71	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
72	
73	from event_ledger import (  # noqa: E402
74	    C_ABSTRACT, C_CITATION, C_FULLTEXT, C_NOT_DOC, C_UNREADABLE,
75	    EventKind, Ledger, derive_content_profile, observe_text,
76	)
77	
78	#: THE ONE DURABLE LEDGER. The run orchestrator opens it BEFORE retrieval; every fetcher appends.
79	LEDGER_PATH = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'event_ledger.jsonl'
80	#: THE IMMUTABLE BLOB STORE. Content-addressed => a blob can never be overwritten, only re-written
81	#: identically. There is no "update" operation and no way to ask for one.
82	BLOB_DIR = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'blobs'
83	
84	MAILTO = os.environ.get('POLARIS_MAILTO', 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com')
85	UA = {'User-Agent': f'POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:{MAILTO})'}
86	
87	#: Politeness spacing, PER HOST — we are the ones who got ourselves throttled, and we got throttled by
88	#: hammering two hosts, not "the network". Backoff is scaled by POLARIS_BACKOFF_SCALE so an adversarial
89	#: test can force a 429 storm without sleeping for four minutes. It scales SLEEP ONLY: it cannot change
90	#: an event, a retry count, or an outcome.
91	SPACING_S = float(os.environ.get('POLARIS_SPACING', '1.1'))
92	BACKOFF_SCALE = float(os.environ.get('POLARIS_BACKOFF_SCALE', '1.0'))
93	
94	#: What the transport did. NOT what the world contains — that distinction is the whole module.
95	RESPONDED       = 'RESPONDED'         # the backend answered with content
96	NOT_INDEXED     = 'NOT_INDEXED'       # HTTP 404: a fact about THEIR INDEX
97	THROTTLED       = 'THROTTLED'         # HTTP 429/503: a fact about OUR REQUEST RATE
98	BLOCKED         = 'BLOCKED'           # HTTP 401/403/402/451: a fact about ENTITLEMENT
99	TRANSPORT_ERROR = 'TRANSPORT_ERROR'   # timeout / DNS / reset / 5xx: we never got an answer
100	
101	THROTTLE_CODES = (429, 503)
102	BLOCK_CODES    = (401, 402, 403, 451)
103	
104	
105	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
106	# THE IMMUTABLE BLOB STORE
107	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
108	
109	class BlobStore:
110	    """Content-addressed, write-once. `put` is idempotent BY CONSTRUCTION, not by convention."""
111	
112	    def __init__(self, root: Path | str | None = None):
113	        # Resolved AT CALL TIME, not bound as a default at def time — a test that redirects the store
114	        # to a temp dir must not silently write to the production one.
115	        self.root = Path(root) if root is not None else Path(BLOB_DIR)
116	        self.root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
117	
118	    def _path(self, digest: str) -> Path:
119	        return self.root / digest[:2] / f'{digest}.bin'
120	
121	    def put(self, raw: bytes) -> tuple[str, str]:
122	        """-> (blob_id, sha256). Writing the same bytes twice writes the same blob, always."""
123	        digest = hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest()
124	        p = self._path(digest)
125	        if not p.exists():
126	            p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
127	            tmp = p.with_suffix('.tmp')
128	            tmp.write_bytes(raw)
129	            tmp.replace(p)                     # atomic: a half-written blob is never a readable blob
130	        return f'sha256:{digest}', digest
131	
132	    def put_text(self, text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
133	        return self.put((text or '').encode('utf-8'))
134	
135	    def get(self, blob_id: str) -> bytes:
136	        digest = blob_id.split(':', 1)[-1]
137	        p = self._path(digest)
138	        if not p.exists():
139	            raise FileNotFoundError(f'no blob {blob_id!r} — the bytes a manifestation names are gone')
140	        raw = p.read_bytes()
141	        if hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest() != digest:
142	            raise ValueError(f'blob {blob_id!r} DOES NOT HASH TO ITS OWN NAME — the store is corrupt')
143	        return raw
144	
145	    def get_text(self, blob_id: str) -> str:
146	        return self.get(blob_id).decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
147	
148	    def has(self, blob_id: str) -> bool:
149	        return self._path(blob_id.split(':', 1)[-1]).exists()
150	
151	
152	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
153	# THE ONE EXTRACTOR — four fetchers had four copies of this, and they disagreed
154	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
155	
156	_TAG = re.compile(r'<[^>]+>')
157	
158	
159	def extract_text(raw: bytes, content_type: str = '') -> tuple[str, str]:
160	    """(text, method). PDF via pdfminer, HTML via tag-stripping. Verbatim: NOTHING is summarised here.
161	
162	    An extraction FAILURE returns ('', 'pdf_unparseable') — never ''-meaning-"no document exists".
163	    The caller records the observation; the reducer decides what it means.
164	    """
165	    if not raw:
166	        return '', 'empty_body'
167	    if 'pdf' in (content_type or '').lower() or raw[:4] == b'%PDF':
168	        try:
169	            from pdfminer.high_level import extract_text as _pdf
170	            return (_pdf(io.BytesIO(raw)) or ''), 'pdfminer'
171	        except Exception as e:
172	            return '', f'pdf_unparseable ({type(e).__name__})'
173	    try:
174	        html = raw.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
175	    except Exception:
176	        return '', 'undecodable_bytes'
177	    html = re.sub(r'(?is)<(script|style|nav|header|footer|aside)[^>]*>.*?</\1>', ' ', html)
178	    txt = re.sub(r'&[a-z]+;', ' ', _TAG.sub(' ', html))
179	    return re.sub(r'\s{2,}', ' ', txt).strip(), 'html_strip'
180	
181	
182	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
183	# THE TYPED RESPONSE — `None` is four different worlds, and it is not one of them any more
184	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
185	
186	@dataclass(frozen=True)
187	class Response:
188	    """What came back. `outcome` is an OBSERVATION about the transport, never about the literature.
189	
190	    THERE IS NO `.absent` AND THERE NEVER WILL BE. The whole disease was one line — `if not d: return
191	    []` — in which four transport outcomes became a claim about the world. A caller may branch on
192	    `.ok`; if it wants to know whether a copy EXISTS it must ask a reducer, over the ledger, which
193	    knows the difference between "they answered, and it is not there" and "we were throttled".
194	    """
195	    outcome: str
196	    http_status: int | None
197	    raw: bytes
198	    content_type: str
199	    url: str
200	    adapter: str
201	    request_id: str
202	    transport_error: str = ''
203	
204	    @property
205	    def ok(self) -> bool:
206	        return self.outcome == RESPONDED
207	
208	    def json(self) -> Any | None:
209	        """The parsed body, or None — and a None HERE means UNPARSEABLE JSON, nothing else."""
210	        if not self.ok:
211	            return None
212	        try:
213	            return json.loads(self.raw)
214	        except Exception:
215	            return None
216	
217	    def text(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
218	        return extract_text(self.raw, self.content_type) if self.ok else ('', 'no_response')
219	
220	
221	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
222	# THE ACQUIRER
223	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
224	
225	_HOST_LAST: dict[str, float] = {}
226	_RID = itertools.count(1)
227	
228	
229	def open_ledger(path: Path | str | None = None) -> Ledger:
230	    """THE ONE DURABLE LEDGER, opened AS IT STANDS ON DISK. Every fetcher in a run shares it.
231	
232	    `Ledger.load` re-reads the history, so a fetcher started after another fetcher finished REDUCES
233	    OVER WHAT HAPPENED, not over the handful of events it emitted itself.
234	    """
235	    return Ledger.load(Path(path) if path else LEDGER_PATH)
236	
237	
238	def content_host(url: str) -> str:
239	    """The adapter name for a DOCUMENT fetch. A content host is a backend too: if nber.org 403s us,
240	    we were BLOCKED BY NBER, and that is a fact worth keeping — distinct from "no copy exists"."""
241	    try:
242	        return 'content:' + (urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc or 'unknown')
243	    except Exception:
244	        return 'content:unknown'
245	
246	
247	class Acquirer:
248	    """Emits observations. Cannot emit a status: no method here takes one.
249	
250	    Every method is a thin, boring wrapper over `Ledger.emit`, and that is the point — the interesting
251	    part of this class is what it DOES NOT EXPOSE.
252	    """
253	
254	    def __init__(self, actor: str, ledger: Ledger | None = None, blobs: BlobStore | None = None):
255	        self.actor = actor
256	        self.ledger = ledger if ledger is not None else open_ledger()
257	        self.blobs = blobs if blobs is not None else BlobStore()
258	
259	    # -- 1. ROUTE_PLANNED -------------------------------------------------------------------------
260	    def plan_route(self, unit: str, adapters: Iterable[str], **obs) -> None:
261	        """`route_complete` will mean EVERY ONE OF THESE HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
262	
263	        It must never mean "an adapter was mapped", so this is called BEFORE the loop, with the
264	        adapters we are ABOUT to try — not after, with the ones that happened to answer.
265	        """
266	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, self.actor,
267	                         adapters=list(adapters), **obs)
268	
269	    # -- 2 & 3. THE EXCEPTION BOUNDARY ------------------------------------------------------------
270	    def get(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, *, tries: int = 4, timeout: int = 30,
271	            **obs) -> Response:
272	        """One logical request. BACKEND_ATTEMPTED before each attempt; EXACTLY ONE of
273	        RESPONSE_RECEIVED | THROTTLED | BLOCKED at the exception boundary of each attempt.
274	
275	        THE RETRIES SHARE A `request_id`. That is load-bearing: a 429 that a backoff RESOLVED and a
276	        429 that we GAVE UP ON are different facts, and the reducer must be able to tell them apart
277	        without either of them being deleted from the log. Both are here, forever; the reducer reads
278	        the request's FINAL terminal event.
279	
280	          exhausted 429s -> the last terminal event is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
281	          429 then 200   -> the last terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED. The 429 is
282	                            still on disk, because the backoff working is not the same as it never
283	                            having been throttled.
284	        """
285	        rid = f'{adapter}#{next(_RID)}'
286	        last: Response | None = None
287	        for attempt in range(1, max(1, tries) + 1):
288	            self._space(url)
289	            self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, self.actor,
290	                             adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt, **obs)
291	            try:
292	                req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA)
293	                # NOTE: `urllib.request.urlopen` is resolved AT CALL TIME, on purpose. The adversary
294	                # harness monkeypatches it to force a 429 storm, and a `from ... import urlopen` would
295	                # bind past the patch and test nothing.
296	                with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as r:
297	                    raw = r.read()
298	                    ctype = r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') or ''
299	                    code = int(getattr(r, 'status', 200) or 200)
300	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
301	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
302	                                 http_status=code, content_type=ctype, n_bytes=len(raw))
303	                return Response(RESPONDED, code, raw, ctype, url, adapter, rid)
304	
305	            except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
306	                code = int(getattr(e, 'code', 0) or 0)
307	
308	                if code in THROTTLE_CODES:
309	                    # ** A 429 PERSISTS AS THROTTLED. IT CANNOT BECOME CITATION_ONLY. **
310	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.THROTTLED, self.actor,
311	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
312	                                     http_status=code, retry_after=self._retry_after(e))
313	                    last = Response(THROTTLED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
314	                    if attempt < tries:
315	                        self._backoff(attempt)
316	                        continue
317	                    return last
318	
319	                if code in BLOCK_CODES:
320	                    # A fact about ENTITLEMENT. Retrying a paywall is not politeness, it is noise.
321	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BLOCKED, self.actor,
322	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
323	                                     http_status=code)
324	                    return Response(BLOCKED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
325	
326	                if code == 404:
327	                    # THE BACKEND ANSWERED. "My index does not have this" is a RESPONSE, and it is a
328	                    # fact about THEIR INDEX — not about whether the paper is free somewhere else.
329	                    # (Semantic Scholar 404s the QJE DOI for Autor/Levy/Murnane. NBER WP 8337 exists.)
330	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
331	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
332	                                     http_status=404, n_bytes=0)
333	                    return Response(NOT_INDEXED, 404, b'', '', url, adapter, rid)
334	
335	                # 5xx and the rest: they responded, and the response is not an answer.
336	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
337	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
338	                                 http_status=code, transport_error=f'HTTP {code}')
339	                last = Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
340	                if code >= 500 and attempt < tries:
341	                    self._backoff(attempt)
342	                    continue
343	                return last
344	
345	            except Exception as e:
346	                # A HANG IS NOT A GAP. `transport_error` carries the exception CLASS (a bounded,
347	                # component-authored word); the message goes under `reason_text`, which the ledger's
348	                # conclusion guard treats as raw text from the world — because an exception string is
349	                # free text and could otherwise smuggle "not available" into the log as a conclusion.
350	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
351	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
352	                                 transport_error=type(e).__name__, reason_text=str(e)[:200])
353	                last = Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, None, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, type(e).__name__)
354	                if attempt < tries:
355	                    self._backoff(attempt)
356	                    continue
357	                return last
358	
359	        return last or Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, None, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, 'no attempt made')
360	
361	    def get_json(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, **kw) -> tuple[Response, Any | None]:
362	        r = self.get(unit, adapter, url, **kw)
363	        return r, r.json()
364	
365	    # -- 4. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED ------------------------------------------------------------------
366	    def candidate(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, **obs) -> None:
367	        """An adapter returned a URL. It is a CANDIDATE — not evidence, not a copy of anything yet."""
368	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, self.actor,
369	                         adapter=adapter, url=url, **obs)
370	
371	    # -- 5 & 6. MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, then CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED -------------------------------
372	    def record_manifestation(self, unit: str, *, locator: str, raw: bytes, text: str,
373	                             adapter: str, requested_title: str = '',
374	                             requested_authors: Iterable[str] | None = None,
375	                             requested_doi: str = '', requested_venue: str = '',
376	                             requested_year: int | None = None, source_type: str = '',
377	                             extraction_method: str = '', **obs) -> dict:
378	        """The bytes we hold, pinned to an IMMUTABLE BLOB and its HASH — then profiled by the SHARED
379	        reducer, which is the only thing allowed to look at content.
380	
381	        NOTE WHAT THIS SIGNATURE DOES NOT ACCEPT: a content status, a fulltext_source, a word count, a
382	        `complete` flag, or a profile. The profile is computed HERE, by `observe_text`, from the bytes
383	        that were just stored. A fetcher cannot hand one in, so a fetcher cannot inflate one — which is
384	        exactly how `fulltext_words: 8,000` came to sit beside 535 words of cookie banner.
385	        """
386	        blob_id, byte_sha = self.blobs.put(raw or b'')
387	        text_blob_id, text_sha = self.blobs.put_text(text or '')
388	
389	        self.ledger.emit(
390	            unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, self.actor,
391	            adapter=adapter,
392	            locator=locator,                 # THE URL THESE BYTES CAME FROM. wp_fetch never recorded
393	            #                                # it, so `oa_url` (the PUBLISHER's link, left by an
394	            #                                # earlier fetcher) was the only URL on the row — a locator
395	            #                                # naming aeaweb.org over the bytes of an NBER working paper.
396	            blob_id=blob_id, byte_sha256=byte_sha, n_bytes=len(raw or b''),
397	            text_blob_id=text_blob_id, text_sha256=text_sha,
398	            extraction_method=extraction_method,
399	            # THE IDENTITY WE ASKED FOR — kept beside the bytes, so the reducer can ask whether the
400	            # bytes are the work we requested. (A title search for "Rise of the Machines" returned
401	            # Yang-Hui He's arXiv paper on theorem-proving, and it was filed under an HR journal
402	            # article by Parry et al. Nothing on the row could have caught that; the requested identity
403	            # is what makes it catchable.)
404	            requested_title=requested_title or '',
405	            requested_authors=list(requested_authors or []),
406	            requested_doi=requested_doi or '',
407	            requested_venue=requested_venue or '',
408	            requested_year=requested_year,
409	            source_type=source_type or '',
410	            **obs)
411	
412	        # ---- 6. THE SHARED ARTIFACT-PROFILE REDUCER. The caller supplies no part of this. ---------
413	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text',
414	                         **observe_text(text or ''))
415	
416	        return dict(blob_id=blob_id, byte_sha256=byte_sha,
417	                    text_blob_id=text_blob_id, text_sha256=text_sha)
418	
419	    # -- a budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP -----------------------------------------------------
420	    def budget_stopped(self, unit: str, **obs) -> None:
421	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED, self.actor, **obs)
422	
423	    # -- politeness ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
424	    @staticmethod
425	    def _retry_after(e: urllib.error.HTTPError) -> str:
426	        try:
427	            return str(e.headers.get('Retry-After') or '') if e.headers else ''
428	        except Exception:
429	            return ''
430	
431	    @staticmethod
432	    def _space(url: str) -> None:
433	        host = urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc or 'unknown'
434	        wait = _HOST_LAST.get(host, 0.0) + SPACING_S - time.time()
435	        if wait > 0:
436	            time.sleep(wait)
437	        _HOST_LAST[host] = time.time()
438	
439	    @staticmethod
440	    def _backoff(attempt: int) -> None:
441	        d = 3.0 * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * BACKOFF_SCALE
442	        if d > 0:
443	            time.sleep(d)
444	
445	
446	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
447	# WHAT DO WE ALREADY HOLD? — DERIVED from the bytes, never read off a label
448	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
449	
450	def held_content_class(row: dict) -> tuple[str, dict]:
451	    """The content class the ROW'S OWN BYTES earn, through the shared reducer.
452	
453	    THIS REPLACES `row['content_status'] == 'CITATION_ONLY'` AS THE TARGET SELECTOR, and the
454	    replacement is not cosmetic. Selecting targets by the label meant:
455	
456	      * deep_fetch NEVER RETRIED the 535-word aeaweb cookie banner, because the label on it said
457	        FULLTEXT. The label was written by the fetcher that failed to get the paper.
458	      * wp_fetch NEVER RETRIED the paper whose PDF decoded to pure (cid:NN) glyph codes, for the
459	        same reason.
460	
461	    A selector that reads a component's own claim about its own success can only ever confirm it.
462	    """
463	    L = Ledger()                                    # scratch, in-memory: this derives, it does not log
464	    unit = row.get('doi') or row.get('title') or '?'
465	    text = (row.get('fulltext') or '').strip()
466	    if text:
467	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'corpus',
468	               locator=row.get('oa_url') or '',
469	               requested_title=row.get('title') or '',
470	               requested_authors=list(row.get('authors') or []),
471	               source_type=str(row.get('type') or ''))
472	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **observe_text(text))
473	    return derive_content_profile(L.events(unit))
474	
475	
476	def holds_usable_document(row: dict) -> bool:
477	    """Do we hold a COMPLETE document for this row? (not: does a label say so)"""
478	    return held_content_class(row)[0] == C_FULLTEXT
479	
480	
481	def holds_nothing(row: dict) -> bool:
482	    """Do we hold no usable document at all — no bytes, or bytes that are not a document?
483	
484	    C_NOT_DOC (a cookie banner) and C_UNREADABLE (a PDF whose font never decoded) belong HERE, with
485	    C_CITATION. Under the old label they were `FULLTEXT` and were never fetched again.
486	    """
487	    cls, _ = held_content_class(row)
488	    if cls in (C_NOT_DOC, C_UNREADABLE):
489	        return True
490	    return cls == C_CITATION and not (row.get('abstract') or '').strip()
491	
492	
493	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
494	# MERGING EVENT STREAMS — an append-only log is merged by UNION, never by a winner
495	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
496	
497	def _event_identity(d: dict) -> str:
498	    """What makes two records THE SAME OBSERVATION. Not `seq` — that is local to one log."""
499	    return json.dumps([d.get('unit'), d.get('kind'), d.get('actor'), round(float(d.get('ts') or 0), 4),
500	                       d.get('payload')], sort_keys=True)
501	
502	
503	def merge_ledgers(paths: Iterable[Path | str], out: Path | str) -> tuple[int, int]:
504	    """Union of append-only logs, re-sequenced. -> (n_events, n_duplicates_dropped).
505	
506	    Two fetchers running against one file made the LAST WRITER WIN and silently flatten the other's
507	    work. Two fetchers appending to one LOG cannot: an observation is not a slot to be overwritten,
508	    it is a record that happened. The only merge an append-only log admits is a union.
509	    """
510	    seen: set[str] = set()
511	    rows: list[dict] = []
512	    dupes = 0
513	    for p in paths:
514	        p = Path(p)
515	        if not p.exists():
516	            continue
517	        for line in p.read_text().splitlines():
518	            line = line.strip()
519	            if not line:
520	                continue
521	            d = json.loads(line)
522	            key = _event_identity(d)
523	            if key in seen:
524	                dupes += 1
525	                continue
526	            seen.add(key)
527	            rows.append(d)
528	    rows.sort(key=lambda d: (float(d.get('ts') or 0), int(d.get('seq') or 0)))
529	    out = Path(out)
530	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
531	    with open(out, 'w') as fh:
532	        for i, d in enumerate(rows, 1):
533	            d['seq'] = i                            # re-sequenced: seq is an ORDER, not an identity
534	            fh.write(json.dumps(d, sort_keys=True) + '\n')
535	    return len(rows), dupes
536	
537	
538	def manifestations_of(ledger: Ledger, unit: str) -> list[dict]:
539	    """Every distinct set of bytes we have EVER held for this work, newest last. NOTHING IS DELETED.
540	
541	    Deduped on `text_sha256` — the content address. Fetching the same document twice is one
542	    manifestation; fetching two DIFFERENT documents for one DOI is two, and the graph keeps both,
543	    because "which of these is the paper" is a question for a reducer with evidence, not for whichever
544	    fetcher wrote the file last.
545	    """
546	    out: dict[str, dict] = {}
547	    for e in ledger.events(unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED):
548	        if 'derived_by' in e.payload:
549	            continue
550	        h = e.payload.get('text_sha256') or e.payload.get('byte_sha256')
551	        if not h:
552	            continue
553	        out[h] = {**e.payload, 'seq': e.seq, 'actor': e.actor}
554	    return list(out.values())
555	
556	
557	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
558	# THE EVIDENCE SELECTION REDUCER — which of the bytes we hold is THE DOCUMENT?
559	#
560	# THE OLD ANSWER WAS "THE LONGEST ONE" (merge_corpus.py:47-54, and a `if len(t.split()) >= 500: break`
561	# in every fetcher). Length is not a property that distinguishes a paper from a cookie banner: the
562	# aeaweb.org landing page for Autor (2015) is 535 words and the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne is
563	# 548, and BOTH of them beat a real 400-word abstract on length while being no document at all.
564	#
565	# The answer here is: the one the SHARED REDUCER says is a complete, finding-bearing document — and
566	# among those, the most AUTHORITATIVE VERSION, which is a question about peer review, not about size.
567	# Length breaks ties only between documents already agreed to be the same kind of thing.
568	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
569	
570	#: How much authority a version carries. `journal_version` outranks `working_paper` because PEER REVIEW
571	#: CHANGES NUMBERS — Acemoglu-Restrepo robots-and-jobs is 0.37pp in NBER WP 23285 and 0.2pp in the
572	#: published JPE. It is not a preference. They are different documents with different numbers in them.
573	_VERSION_AUTHORITY = {
574	    'journal_version': 6, 'official_text': 6, 'registry_record': 6,
575	    'proceedings_version': 5, 'accepted_manuscript': 4,
576	    'working_paper': 3, 'preprint': 2, 'unknown': 1,
577	}
578	
579	#: The content classes that can be a document at all, best first.
580	_CLASS_RANK = {C_FULLTEXT: 3, C_ABSTRACT: 2, C_CITATION: 1, C_NOT_DOC: 0, C_UNREADABLE: 0}
581	
582	
583	def classify_manifestation(m: dict, blobs: BlobStore) -> dict:
584	    """Run ONE manifestation's bytes back through the shared reducer. Concludes nothing on its own."""
585	    from provenance import derive_expression_kind      # local: provenance imports are not free
586	
587	    text = blobs.get_text(m['text_blob_id']) if m.get('text_blob_id') else ''
588	    L = Ledger()
589	    L.emit('m', EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'select', **{
590	        k: v for k, v in m.items()
591	        if k in ('locator', 'requested_title', 'requested_authors', 'source_type', 'adapter')})
592	    L.emit('m', EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **observe_text(text))
593	    cls, info = derive_content_profile(L.events('m'))
594	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text)
595	    return dict(
596	        manifestation=m, text=text, content_class=cls, artifact_kind=info.get('artifact_kind'),
597	        is_complete=bool(info.get('complete')), expression_kind=ekind, expression_basis=ebasis,
598	        readable_words=info.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0,
599	        basis=info.get('reason', ''),
600	        rank=(_CLASS_RANK.get(cls, 0), _VERSION_AUTHORITY.get(ekind, 0),
601	              info.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0))
602	
603	
604	def select_evidence(ledger: Ledger, unit: str, blobs: BlobStore | None = None) -> dict | None:
605	    """Which manifestation is THE DOCUMENT for this work? -> the winner, with its basis. Or None.
606	
607	    EVERY CANDIDATE SURVIVES THIS CALL. Selection is a VIEW, not a deletion: the losers keep their
608	    blobs, their hashes and their events, and the provenance graph carries every one of them. "Which
609	    of these is the paper" is a question a reducer answers with evidence, and it is allowed to be
610	    answered differently tomorrow, on the same bytes, by a better reducer.
611	    """
612	    blobs = blobs or BlobStore()
613	    cands = [classify_manifestation(m, blobs) for m in manifestations_of(ledger, unit)]
614	    if not cands:
615	        return None
616	    return max(cands, key=lambda c: c['rank'])
617	
618	
619	if __name__ == '__main__':
620	    print(__doc__)
621	    print('This module is a library. Its invariants are tested by:  '
622	          'python3 scripts/test_acquisition_observes.py')
623

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE QUERY COMPILER — compiles ANY question into a RESEARCH CONTRACT.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	---------------
6	cellcog_composer.py is a TASK-72 MACHINE. The extraction question (:103), the outline (:264), the
7	title and abstract (:744), the sector list, the unit-of-analysis enum (:140) and the rubric reading
8	(:250) are all hardcoded. Every one of our 38 scored runs is task 72. WE DO NOT KNOW WHAT THIS
9	PIPELINE SCORES ON AN UNSEEN QUESTION, and the mission is a system that beats SOTA on ANY question.
10	
11	This module is the layer that was missing: question in, CONTRACT out. Everything the composer needs
12	to be told about a topic -- what to mine, what to compare, what to cover, what genre to write, which
13	sources are admissible -- is DERIVED HERE, and nothing about any particular topic is written down.
14	
15	    compile_contract(question)      -> Contract          ONE LLM call, cached on disk, span-gated
16	    coverage_matrix(contract, cards)-> Matrix            pure function
17	    derive_outline(contract, matrix)-> list[Slot]        pure function
18	    build_extract_prompt(...)       -> str               replaces the hardcoded EXTRACT_PROMPT
19	
20	THE ONE LLM CALL IS THE ONLY NONDETERMINISM. Coverage, outline, facets, card allocation and prompts
21	are pure derivations from the compiled contract, so they are reproducible, auditable, and testable
22	without a network. `--no-llm` compiles a degraded contract from the question by regex alone, so CI
23	can exercise every derivation offline.
24	
25	THE COMPILER IS ITSELF SPAN-GATED
26	---------------------------------
27	The same law that governs the report governs the contract. A SOURCE CONSTRAINT is an ATTRIBUTED
28	claim -- it is attributed to THE QUESTION -- so it must be entailed by a VERBATIM SPAN OF THE
29	QUESTION. The model must quote the clause that imposes each constraint; a constraint whose quote is
30	not literally in the question is DROPPED, never repaired. And because a model that forgets a
31	constraint is as dangerous as one that invents one, a REGEX FLOOR independently detects the
32	constraints that are cheap to detect, and the model may ADD to that floor but may never DROP it.
33	
34	    Q: "Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."
35	    -> peer_reviewed_only=True, languages=['English'], and neither can be argued away.
36	
37	WHAT A CELL OF THE COVERAGE MATRIX IS
38	-------------------------------------
39	rows    = the SUBJECT AXIS (industries for one question, disease stages for another, liability
40	          scenarios for a third -- THE AXIS IS NAMED BY THE CONTRACT, not by this file)
41	columns = the OUTCOME DIMENSIONS
42	
43	A cell CLOSES on evidence:      >=2 groundable works, >=1 result, method contrast where material
44	A cell CLOSES on an honest GAP: an explicit, corpus-scoped "the literature we hold does not cover
45	                                this" -- which is worth MORE than filler, and is the direct fix for
46	                                the outline writing cheques the corpus cannot cash (the task-72
47	                                outline promised a 4-subsection industry section over a corpus
48	                                holding retail=0, education=1).
49	
50	Usage:
51	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
52	    python scripts/research_contract.py                      # compiles task 72, prints everything
53	    python scripts/research_contract.py --task 90            # any DeepResearch Bench task
54	    python scripts/research_contract.py --question "..."     # any question at all
55	    python scripts/research_contract.py --no-llm             # offline, regex-only contract
56	    python scripts/research_contract.py --audit-rubric       # POST-HOC only. never an input.
57	"""

79	# THE COMPILER VERSION. It is part of the cache key, so a contract compiled by an older prompt OR an
80	# older gate can never be silently reused. Bump it when the prompt, the schema, OR the admission
81	# logic changes -- the cache stores the POST-GATE contract, so a loosened gate lives on in the cache
82	# until this changes. (rc-2 cached a fabricated `recency_from: 2015`.)

94	@dataclass
95	class Term:
96	    """A concept the matcher can look for: a label plus the surface forms it wears."""
97	    key: str
98	    label: str
99	    aliases: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
100	
101	
102	@dataclass
103	class Axis:
104	    """The rows of the coverage matrix. THE GENERALISATION OF 'INDUSTRIES'.
105	
106	    For a question about AI and work this is `Industry`. For one about Parkinson's it is `Disease
107	    stage`. For one about ADAS liability it is `Accident scenario`. The composer must never assume.
108	    """
109	    name: str = 'Subpopulation'
110	    values: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
111	
112	
113	@dataclass
114	class Contrast:
115	    """A tension the review is OBLIGED to draw. This is the shared argument state that 28
116	    independently-generated subsections did not have, and that no later cohesion pass can invent."""
117	    key: str
118	    label: str
119	    a: str
120	    b: str
121	    why: str = ''

124	@dataclass
125	class Facet:
126	    """An extraction facet the miner harvests. Derived from the question -- for task 72 one of these
127	    is the Fourth Industrial Revolution, but NOTHING about the 4IR is written in this file."""
128	    key: str
129	    label: str
130	    probe: str            # the question asked of every paper
131	    priority: int = 2     # 1 = must-have
132	    serves: str = ''      # which contract element it feeds
133	
134	
135	@dataclass
136	class SourcePolicy:
137	    """The REQUESTED SOURCE CONSTRAINTS. Every field must point at a verbatim clause of the question."""
138	    peer_reviewed_only: bool = False
139	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
140	    excluded_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
141	    quality_bar: str = ''
142	    recency_from: int | None = None
143	    question_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # VERBATIM spans of the question
144	
145	    def compliance_prose(self) -> str:
146	        """What the Scope & Methods section must SAY. On a question graded 'only cites high-quality
147	        journal articles', the winning system EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER in prose and we
148	        currently say nothing.
149	
150	        DELIBERATELY NUMBER-FREE. A sentence about our own procedure names no source, so the
151	        composer's gate reads it as OWNED -- and an OWNED sentence carrying a number is dropped
152	        (OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER). Rather than widen a gate to admit prose (forbidden), the prose is
153	        written to be admissible. Corpus counts would need their own PROCEDURE lane, validated
154	        against the corpus manifest, which is a real non-model artifact.
155	        """
156	        bits = []
157	        if self.peer_reviewed_only:
158	            bits.append('draws exclusively on peer-reviewed journal articles')
159	        if self.quality_bar:
160	            bits.append(f'applies a {self.quality_bar} standard to every source admitted')
161	        if self.languages:
162	            langs = ' and '.join(self.languages)
163	            bits.append(f'admits only articles published in {langs}')
164	        if self.recency_from:
165	            bits.append(f'restricts the evidence base to work published from {self.recency_from} onward')
166	        if self.excluded_types:
167	            ex = ', '.join(self.excluded_types[:5])
168	            bits.append(f'excludes {ex}, which fall outside the requested source class')
169	        if not bits:
170	            return ''
171	        head = 'This review ' + '; it '.join(bits) + '.'
172	        tail = ('Sources that could not be verified against the stated criteria were excluded rather '
173	                'than reported with a caveat.')
174	        return head + ' ' + tail
175	
176	
177	@dataclass
178	class Contract:
179	    """THE RESEARCH CONTRACT. Everything the composer is allowed to know about the topic."""
180	    question: str
181	    question_id: int | None = None
182	    genre: str = 'literature review'
183	    genre_rules: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
184	    review_subject: str = ''
185	    title: str = ''
186	    core_concepts: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
187	    framing_devices: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)   # a lens the QUESTION imposes
188	    subject_axis: Axis = field(default_factory=Axis)
189	    outcome_dimensions: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
190	    geographies: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
191	    time_horizons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
192	    method_designs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # the design vocabulary FOR THIS Q
193	    unit_levels: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)        # the `level` enum FOR THIS Q
194	    required_contrasts: list[Contrast] = field(default_factory=list)
195	    source_policy: SourcePolicy = field(default_factory=SourcePolicy)
196	    facets: list[Facet] = field(default_factory=list)
197	    evidence_tuple: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
198	    compiled_by: str = ''
199	    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
200	
201	    def to_json(self) -> str:
202	        return json.dumps(asdict(self), indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
203	
204	    @staticmethod
205	    def from_dict(d: dict) -> 'Contract':
206	        t = lambda xs: [Term(**x) for x in xs or []]
207	        c = Contract(**{k: v for k, v in d.items() if k not in (
208	            'core_concepts', 'framing_devices', 'subject_axis', 'outcome_dimensions',
209	            'required_contrasts', 'source_policy', 'facets')})
210	        c.core_concepts = t(d.get('core_concepts'))
211	        c.framing_devices = t(d.get('framing_devices'))
212	        ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
213	        c.subject_axis = Axis(name=ax.get('name', 'Subpopulation'), values=t(ax.get('values')))
214	        c.outcome_dimensions = t(d.get('outcome_dimensions'))
215	        c.required_contrasts = [Contrast(**x) for x in d.get('required_contrasts') or []]
216	        c.source_policy = SourcePolicy(**(d.get('source_policy') or {}))
217	        c.facets = [Facet(**x) for x in d.get('facets') or []]
218	        return c

220	
221	# ===================================================================== the regex floor
222	#
223	# THE MODEL MAY ADD TO THIS. IT MAY NEVER DROP IT. A source constraint is 0.25 of instruction-
224	# following on task 72 (journal-only 0.15 + English-only 0.10); a compiler that can be talked out of
225	# a constraint by its own LLM is not a compiler.
226	
227	_FLOOR = [
228	    # (regex over the question, field, value)
229	    (r'peer[-\s]?reviewed|journal article|scholarly (?:article|literature|source)|'
230	     r'academic (?:journal|literature)|published (?:in )?journals?', 'peer_reviewed_only', True),
231	    (r'english[-\s]?language|in english', 'language', 'English'),
232	    (r'high[-\s]?quality|top[-\s]?tier|leading journals?|reputable', 'quality_bar', 'high-quality'),
233	]
234	_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED = ['books', 'conference proceedings', 'preprints', 'working papers',
235	                             'news articles', 'blog posts', 'non-peer-reviewed reports']
236	
237	_GENRE_FLOOR = [
238	    (r'literature review|review the literature|systematic review', 'literature review'),
239	    (r'policy brief', 'policy brief'),
240	    (r'comparative analysis|compare and contrast', 'comparative analysis'),
241	    (r'\banalys[ei]s?\b.*\brecommendations?\b|propose[d]? (?:regulatory )?guidelines',
242	     'analytical report with recommendations'),
243	    (r'\breport\b', 'report'),
244	]
245	
246	_RECENCY = re.compile(r'(?:since|after|from|published in or after|past\s+\d+\s+years?[, ]*since)\s+'
247	                      r'((?:19|20)\d\d)', re.I)
248	
249	
250	def _floor_source_policy(q: str) -> SourcePolicy:
251	    """Detect the constraints that are cheap to detect. The LLM cannot argue these away."""
252	    sp = SourcePolicy()
253	    for rx, fld, val in _FLOOR:
254	        m = re.search(rx, q, re.I)
255	        if not m:
256	            continue
257	        # THE CLAUSE, VERBATIM. A constraint that cannot point at its clause is not a constraint.
258	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
259	        if fld == 'peer_reviewed_only':
260	            sp.peer_reviewed_only = True
261	        elif fld == 'language':
262	            if val not in sp.languages:
263	                sp.languages.append(val)
264	        elif fld == 'quality_bar':
265	            sp.quality_bar = val
266	    m = _RECENCY.search(q)
267	    if m:
268	        sp.recency_from = int(m.group(1))
269	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
270	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only:
271	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
272	    sp.question_evidence = _dedup(sp.question_evidence)
273	    return sp
274	
275	
276	def _clause_around(q: str, i: int, j: int) -> str:
277	    """The verbatim clause of the question containing [i:j). This is the SPAN a constraint cites."""
278	    lo = max((q.rfind(c, 0, i) for c in '.;\n'), default=-1)
279	    hi = min([x for x in (q.find(c, j) for c in '.;\n') if x != -1] or [len(q)])
280	    return q[lo + 1:hi + 1].strip()
281	
282	
283	def _floor_genre(q: str) -> str:
284	    for rx, g in _GENRE_FLOOR:
285	        if re.search(rx, q, re.I):
286	            return g
287	    return 'analytical report'
288

290	# ===================================================================== the compile prompt
291	#
292	# NOTE ON LEAKAGE: the worked example below is deliberately from a domain UNRELATED to any benchmark
293	# task (urban transit). Putting an AI/labour example here would hardcode task 72 through the back
294	# door -- the model would pattern-match the example instead of reading the question, and the
295	# generality this module exists to provide would be fake.
296	
297	COMPILE_PROMPT = """You are compiling a RESEARCH CONTRACT from a research question. The contract is the ONLY
298	thing a downstream research pipeline will be told about this topic: if a requirement is not in the
299	contract, the pipeline cannot honour it.
300	
301	THE QUESTION (verbatim):
302	---
303	{question}
304	---
305	
306	Return ONE JSON object with exactly these fields:
307	
308	{{
309	 "genre": "the artifact the question asks for -- e.g. literature review / policy brief / comparative
310	           analysis / analytical report with recommendations / clinical guidance. Read the question.",
311	 "genre_rules": ["3-6 obligations that genre imposes on the writer, e.g. for a literature review:
312	                  'synthesise published work rather than present new empirical findings'"],
313	 "review_subject": "the subject of the artifact as a noun phrase, <=12 words, no verb",
314	 "title": "an academic title for the artifact",
315	
316	 "core_concepts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"the concept","aliases":["surface forms a paper
317	                     would actually use, lowercase, 3-8 of them"]}}],
318	
319	 "framing_devices": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a LENS THE QUESTION EXPLICITLY IMPOSES on the
320	                       analysis (a comparison it demands, a context it names, a frame it requires).
321	                       If the question names one, it is graded. If it names none, return [].",
322	                       "aliases":["..."], "why":"what the question obliges you to do with it"}}],
323	
324	 "subject_axis": {{"name":"the name of the axis the question asks you to vary the analysis ACROSS --
325	                    e.g. Industry, Disease stage, Intervention type, Jurisdiction, Population group.
326	                    Choose the ONE axis the question actually demands breadth on.",
327	                   "values":[{{"key":"snake_case","label":"...","aliases":["..."]}}]}},
328	
329	 "outcome_dimensions": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"an OUTCOME the question asks about -- the
330	                          things that get measured or affected","aliases":["..."]}}],
331	
332	 "geographies": ["geographies the question scopes to, or [] if unscoped"],
333	 "time_horizons": ["the time horizons over which effects must be distinguished, e.g. short-run /
334	                   long-run, or acute / chronic. 2-4 items."],
335	 "method_designs": ["the STUDY DESIGNS that would count as evidence in THIS field, e.g.
336	                    randomized-trial / cohort / quasi-experimental / observational / survey /
337	                    case-law analysis / simulation / theory / review. 4-7 items."],
338	 "unit_levels": ["the UNITS OF ANALYSIS results in this field are reported at, from most granular to
339	                 least, e.g. task/worker/firm/economy or cell/patient/clinic/health-system. 4-7."],
340	
341	 "required_contrasts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a tension the artifact is OBLIGED to draw",
342	                          "a":"one side","b":"the other side","why":"why the question demands it"}}],
343	
344	 "source_policy": {{"peer_reviewed_only": true/false,
345	                   "languages": ["English"] or [],
346	                   "excluded_types": ["source types the question forbids"],
347	                   "quality_bar": "the quality wording the question uses, or \\"\\"",
348	                   "recency_from": 2015 or null,
349	                   "question_evidence": ["FOR EACH constraint above, the clause of THE QUESTION that
350	                     imposes it, COPIED VERBATIM. If you cannot copy a clause, the constraint is not
351	                     in the question and MUST NOT be asserted."]}},
352	
353	 "facets": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a facet a miner must harvest from every source",
354	              "probe":"the question to ask of each paper","priority":1|2|3,
355	              "serves":"which contract element this feeds"}}],
356	
357	 "evidence_tuple": ["the fields that make a finding INTERPRETABLE in this field -- typically effect,
358	                    unit, population, design, scope, uncertainty. Adapt to the field."]
359	}}
360	
361	RULES
362	- DERIVE EVERYTHING FROM THE QUESTION. Do not import a template from another topic.
363	- If the question names a framing device ("as a key driver of X", "in the context of Y", "under
364	  condition Z"), that is GRADED. Put it in framing_devices and give it a facet.
365	- subject_axis: 5-9 values, and they must be the ones a literature in THIS field would actually be
366	  organised by. If the question demands breadth across nothing, return values: [].
367	- outcome_dimensions: 4-8. These are the columns of a coverage matrix; they must be distinct.
368	- aliases: THE WORDS PAPERS IN THIS FIELD ACTUALLY PRINT. A deterministic lexical matcher looks for
369	  these in the VERBATIM TEXT of journal articles, so give SURFACE FORMS, not restatements of the
370	  concept. Include: single words as well as phrases; the field's technical vocabulary; the terms the
371	  SEMINAL papers on this topic use; spelling variants (including British spellings). 6-12 per entry,
372	  lowercase, no punctuation. Prefer words that separate this entry from its SIBLINGS -- a word shared
373	  by every entry (like "job" across several outcome dimensions) routes nothing and is discarded.
374	  A concept whose aliases do not literally occur in real papers is INVISIBLE to the pipeline.
375	- source_policy.question_evidence MUST be verbatim substrings of the question. A constraint whose
376	  clause you cannot quote is DROPPED downstream.
377	
378	WORKED EXAMPLE OF THE SHAPE (a different topic entirely -- do not copy its content):
379	  question: "Assess how bus rapid transit affects commuter outcomes in Latin American cities."
380	  genre: "analytical report"; review_subject: "bus rapid transit and commuter outcomes"
381	  subject_axis: {{"name":"City","values":[{{"key":"bogota","label":"Bogota","aliases":["bogota","transmilenio"]}}, ...]}}
382	  outcome_dimensions: [{{"key":"travel_time","label":"Travel time","aliases":["commute time","journey time"]}}, ...]
383	  framing_devices: []  (the question imposes no explicit lens)
384	
385	Return ONLY the JSON object."""
386	
387	
388	# ===================================================================== compile
389	
390	def _llm(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 8192) -> str:
391	    """The composer's LLM helper. Imported LAZILY so that a future `import research_contract` at the
392	    top of cellcog_composer.py cannot create a circular import at module load."""
393	    from cellcog_composer import llm
394	    return llm(prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens)
395	
396	
397	def _jparse(s: str):
398	    from cellcog_composer import jparse
399	    return jparse(s)
400	
401	
402	def _dedup(xs) -> list:
403	    out, seen = [], set()
404	    for x in xs:
405	        k = x.lower().strip() if isinstance(x, str) else str(x)
406	        if k and k not in seen:
407	            seen.add(k)
408	            out.append(x.strip() if isinstance(x, str) else x)
409	    return out
410	
411	
412	_STOP = {'the', 'and', 'for', 'with', 'that', 'this', 'from', 'have', 'has', 'are', 'its', 'their',
413	         'other', 'various', 'such', 'these', 'those', 'more', 'most', 'also', 'into', 'across'}
414	
415	
416	def _terms(raw, cap: int = 32) -> list[Term]:
417	    """Sanitise LLM-supplied terms into a deterministic matcher vocabulary."""
418	    out = []
419	    for d in (raw or [])[:cap]:
420	        if not isinstance(d, dict):
421	            continue
422	        label = (d.get('label') or '').strip()
423	        if not label:
424	            continue
425	        key = (d.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', label.lower()).strip('_'))[:48]
426	        al = [re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', str(a).lower().strip()) for a in (d.get('aliases') or [])]
427	        al = [a for a in al if len(a) >= 3 and a not in _STOP]
428	        # the label is always a surface form of itself
429	        al = _dedup([label.lower()] + al)
430	        out.append(Term(key=key, label=label, aliases=al))
431	    return out
432	
433	
434	def _key(question: str, model: str) -> str:
435	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{PROMPT_VERSION}\x00{model}\x00{question}'.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
436	    return h
437	
438	
439	def compile_contract(question: str, question_id: int | None = None, *, use_llm: bool = True,
440	                     force: bool = False, verbose: bool = True) -> Contract:
441	    """Question -> Contract. ONE LLM call, cached. The regex floor is applied AFTER the model, so the
442	    model can enrich the source policy but cannot weaken it."""
443	    import os
444	    model = os.getenv('PG_GENERATOR_MODEL', 'z-ai/glm-5.2') if use_llm else 'none'
445	    CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
446	    path = CACHE / f'{_key(question, model)}.json'
447	    if path.exists() and not force:
448	        if verbose:
449	            print(f'  [contract] cache hit {path.name}', file=sys.stderr)
450	        return Contract.from_dict(json.loads(path.read_text()))
451	
452	    warnings: list[str] = []
453	    d: dict = {}
454	    if use_llm:
455	        raw = _llm(COMPILE_PROMPT.format(question=question.strip()))
456	        d = _jparse(raw) or {}
457	        if not isinstance(d, dict) or not d:
458	            warnings.append('LLM returned no parsable contract; fell back to the regex floor')
459	            d = {}
460	
461	    # ---- source policy: MODEL PROPOSES, QUESTION DISPOSES, FLOOR IS NON-NEGOTIABLE -------------
462	    floor = _floor_source_policy(question)
463	    sp_raw = d.get('source_policy') or {}
464	    qnorm = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', question.lower())
465	    kept_ev, dropped_ev = [], []
466	    for ev in (sp_raw.get('question_evidence') or []):
467	        if isinstance(ev, str) and re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', ev.lower().strip()) in qnorm:
468	            kept_ev.append(ev.strip())
469	        elif ev:
470	            dropped_ev.append(str(ev)[:60])
471	    if dropped_ev:
472	        warnings.append(f'{len(dropped_ev)} source-constraint quote(s) were NOT verbatim in the '
473	                        f'question and were dropped: {dropped_ev[:2]}')
474	
475	    # ---- PER-CONSTRAINT ENTAILMENT. THE HOLE I ALREADY DUG ONCE, IN A NEW PLACE. -----------------
476	    # This block first read `... if kept_ev`, i.e. a constraint was admitted whenever ANY OTHER
477	    # constraint had produced a verbatim quote. On task 72 the model asserted `recency_from: 2015`;
478	    # the question contains no year at all; and it was ADMITTED, because the quote for the
479	    # journal-only rule had verified. A claim validated by the evidence for a DIFFERENT claim is the
480	    # evidence-laundering shape, and it would have silently deleted Autor 2003, Bresnahan 2002 and
481	    # Frey and Osborne 2017 -- the seminal literature -- from our own corpus.
482	    #
483	    # EVERY CONSTRAINT NOW CARRIES ITS OWN PROOF, CHECKED AGAINST THE QUESTION ITSELF.
484	    def _asks_peer_review() -> bool:
485	        return bool(re.search(_FLOOR[0][0], question, re.I))
486	
487	    langs = list(floor.languages)
488	    for l in (sp_raw.get('languages') or []):
489	        if isinstance(l, str) and re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(l)}\b', question, re.I) and l not in langs:
490	            langs.append(l)          # a language is admitted only if the question NAMES it
491	
492	    qb = floor.quality_bar
493	    if not qb:
494	        cand = (sp_raw.get('quality_bar') or '').strip()
495	        if cand and re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', cand.lower()) in re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', qnorm):
496	            qb = cand                # a quality bar is admitted only if its wording is IN the question
497	
498	    rec = floor.recency_from
499	    if rec is None:
500	        cand = sp_raw.get('recency_from')
501	        if isinstance(cand, int) and str(cand) in question:
502	            rec = cand               # a cutoff year is admitted only if THAT YEAR is in the question
503	        elif cand:
504	            warnings.append(f'DROPPED fabricated recency cutoff {cand}: the question names no year. '
505	                            f'It would have deleted every pre-{cand} paper in the corpus.')
506	
507	    sp = SourcePolicy(
508	        peer_reviewed_only=bool(floor.peer_reviewed_only or
509	                                (sp_raw.get('peer_reviewed_only') and _asks_peer_review())),
510	        languages=_dedup(langs),
511	        # excluded_types are ENTAILMENTS of the journal-only rule, not assertions about the question.
512	        # Over-excluding cannot fabricate; it can only narrow the corpus, which is the requested
513	        # direction. So the model may add to this list freely.
514	        excluded_types=_dedup(list(floor.excluded_types) + list(sp_raw.get('excluded_types') or [])),
515	        quality_bar=qb,
516	        recency_from=rec,
517	        question_evidence=_dedup(list(floor.question_evidence) + kept_ev),
518	    )
519	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only and not sp.excluded_types:
520	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
521	
522	    # ---- the rest ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
523	    ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
524	    contrasts = []
525	    for x in (d.get('required_contrasts') or [])[:8]:
526	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
527	            contrasts.append(Contrast(
528	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
529	                label=x['label'], a=str(x.get('a') or ''), b=str(x.get('b') or ''),
530	                why=str(x.get('why') or '')))
531	    facets = []
532	    for x in (d.get('facets') or [])[:14]:
533	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
534	            facets.append(Facet(
535	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
536	                label=x['label'], probe=str(x.get('probe') or x['label']),
537	                priority=int(x.get('priority') or 2), serves=str(x.get('serves') or '')))
538	
539	    c = Contract(
540	        question=question.strip(),
541	        question_id=question_id,
542	        genre=(d.get('genre') or _floor_genre(question)).strip(),
543	        genre_rules=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('genre_rules') or [])])[:8],
544	        review_subject=(d.get('review_subject') or '').strip(),
545	        title=(d.get('title') or '').strip(),
546	        core_concepts=_terms(d.get('core_concepts')),
547	        framing_devices=_terms(d.get('framing_devices')),
548	        subject_axis=Axis(name=(ax.get('name') or 'Subpopulation').strip(),
549	                          values=_terms(ax.get('values'), cap=12)),
550	        outcome_dimensions=_terms(d.get('outcome_dimensions'), cap=10),
551	        geographies=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('geographies') or [])])[:8],
552	        time_horizons=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('time_horizons') or [])])[:5],
553	        method_designs=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('method_designs') or [])])[:8],
554	        unit_levels=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('unit_levels') or [])])[:8],
555	        required_contrasts=contrasts,
556	        source_policy=sp,
557	        facets=facets,
558	        evidence_tuple=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('evidence_tuple') or [])]) or
559	                       ['effect', 'unit', 'population', 'design', 'scope', 'uncertainty'],
560	        compiled_by=f'{PROMPT_VERSION}/{model}',
561	        warnings=warnings,
562	    )
563	
564	    # ---- degraded, offline contract: still usable, and honest about what it is ------------------
565	    if not c.review_subject:
566	        c.review_subject = _fallback_subject(question)
567	        c.warnings.append('review_subject derived by regex (no LLM)')
568	    if not c.title:
569	        c.title = c.review_subject[:1].upper() + c.review_subject[1:]
570	    if not c.outcome_dimensions:
571	        c.warnings.append('NO OUTCOME DIMENSIONS: the coverage matrix will have no columns')
572	    if not c.subject_axis.values:
573	        c.warnings.append(f'NO {c.subject_axis.name.upper()} VALUES: the matrix collapses to the '
574	                          f'cross-cutting row -- the question may simply not demand breadth on an axis')
575	    if not c.facets:
576	        c.warnings.append('NO FACETS: the miner has nothing question-specific to harvest')
577	
578	    path.write_text(c.to_json())
579	    if verbose:
580	        print(f'  [contract] compiled -> {path}', file=sys.stderr)
581	    return c

584	def _fallback_subject(q: str) -> str:
585	    m = re.search(r'(?:literature review|review|analysis|report|brief)\s+(?:on|of|about)\s+(.{5,90}?)[.,;]', q, re.I)
586	    if m:
587	        return m.group(1).strip()
588	    words = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]+", q) if w.lower() not in _STOP]
589	    return ' '.join(words[:10])
