content.text: I now understand the existing need-based router in `src/polaris_graph/discovery/`. My task is a complementary, capability-based router keyed on evidence roles that goes through `acquisition.Acquirer`. Let me read the event_ledger content/coverage vocabulary and the existing EvidenceNeed enum to align my role vocabulary.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""EVENT LEDGER — every status is a PURE REDUCER over an append-only event log.
3	
4	    Sol, item 8: "NO COMPONENT MAY WRITE `complete`, `fulltext`, `no evidence`, `same work`,
5	                  or `high quality` DIRECTLY."
6	
7	THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR
8	
9	  Every defect we have shipped had ONE shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN ITS CONTENT
10	  SUPPORTED, WITH NOTHING CHECKING. Not one announced itself; each read as a fact about the world.
11	
12	      "gate: WIRED"          -> it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
13	      "span-verified"        -> verified by its FIRST 60 CHARACTERS
14	      "still paywalled"      -> we asked by DOI; the free copy is a SEPARATE WORK
15	      "FULLTEXT"             -> 535 words. A cookie banner.
16	      "no free copy exists"  -> we were HTTP 429/404. A fact about OUR REQUEST and THEIR INDEX,
17	                                disguised as a fact about the world.
18	      "working_paper"        -> written by whichever SCRIPT ran, not by what the document IS.
19	
20	  A component that CAN assert its own success will eventually assert it falsely, and nothing will
21	  catch it. So here a component may ONLY EMIT EVENTS — observations, in the vocabulary of the world:
22	  an HTTP status, a word count, a byline, a glyph ratio. The LABEL is DERIVED from those events by a
23	  reducer nobody can bypass, and the label is never stored anywhere a component could overwrite it.
24	
25	THE TWO RULES THAT MAKE IT STRUCTURAL, NOT ADVISORY
26	
27	  1. EMIT-TIME CONCLUSION GUARD. `Ledger.emit()` REJECTS any event whose payload carries a terminal
28	     conclusion — a key like `content_status`, or a value like "FULLTEXT" / "same work" /
29	     "no evidence" / "high quality". You cannot write the label into the ledger even if you try.
30	     Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `span`, `document_title`) is exempt: a fetched
31	     PDF is allowed to contain the word "complete". A COMPONENT is not.
32	
33	  2. LABELS ARE NEVER STORED. They are computed on read by `derive_*` pure functions over the event
34	     list. There is no field to corrupt, no cache to go stale, and no way to "just set it".
35	
36	THE DISTINCTIONS THAT MUST NEVER COLLAPSE (Sol)
37	
38	    HTTP 429/503        -> BACKEND_FAILED.  ** NOT "no evidence exists". **
39	    HTTP 404            -> NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND. A fact about THEIR INDEX. Also not absence.
40	    route_complete      -> EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
41	                           It must NEVER mean "an adapter was mapped."
42	    a budget stop       -> IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP.
43	    absence             -> only an ADEQUATE route (every adapter genuinely answered, none throttled,
44	                           none blocked, no budget stop) can support a scoped absence statement.
45	    "high_quality"      -> FORBIDDEN as a bare label. It renders as its COMPONENTS, each with
46	                           provenance: directness=high, method_quality=low, influence_percentile=0.92.
47	
48	Run it:
49	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py            # self-test + replay of our real history
50	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --replay   # just the replay
51	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --selftest # just the invariants
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import json
56	import re
57	import sys
58	import time
59	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
60	from pathlib import Path
61	from typing import Any, Iterable
62	
63	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
64	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
65	
66	# THE REGISTRY, AND THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. Imported — never re-implemented. This module used to
67	# carry its own `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` while provenance.py used 1,200, and neither knew about the
68	# other. See the headstone at "3. content profile", below.
69	from provenance import (  # noqa: E402
70	    KIND_PROFILE, judge_completeness,
71	    SOURCE_TYPE as _SOURCE_TYPE, WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT as _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT,
72	)
73	
74	CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
75	LEDGER_OUT = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'event_ledger.jsonl'
76	
77	
78	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
79	# EVENTS — the only thing a component may write.
80	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
81	
82	class EventKind:
83	    """Sol's twelve, verbatim, plus the budget stop (which must be distinguishable from a gap)."""
84	    ROUTE_PLANNED             = 'route_planned'
85	    BACKEND_ATTEMPTED         = 'backend_attempted'
86	    RESPONSE_RECEIVED         = 'response_received'
87	    THROTTLED                 = 'throttled'
88	    BLOCKED                   = 'blocked'
89	    CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED      = 'candidate_identified'
90	    MANIFESTATION_FETCHED     = 'manifestation_fetched'
91	    CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED   = 'content_profile_derived'
92	    SEMANTIC_BINDING_DECIDED  = 'semantic_binding_decided'
93	    ELIGIBILITY_DECIDED       = 'eligibility_decided'
94	    WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED = 'weight_components_derived'
95	    COVERAGE_STATUS_DERIVED   = 'coverage_status_derived'
96	    # "a budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP" — so it needs its own event, or it cannot be told apart.
97	    BUDGET_STOPPED            = 'budget_stopped'
98	
99	    ALL = {ROUTE_PLANNED, BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED, THROTTLED, BLOCKED,
100	           CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED,
101	           SEMANTIC_BINDING_DECIDED, ELIGIBILITY_DECIDED, WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED,
102	           COVERAGE_STATUS_DERIVED, BUDGET_STOPPED}
103	
104	
105	class ForbiddenLabel(Exception):
106	    """A component tried to write a conclusion into the ledger. That is the whole disease."""
107	
108	
109	#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
110	RESERVED_VALUES = (
111	    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
112	    'complete', 'route_complete',
113	    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
114	    'same work', 'same_work', 'version equivalent',
115	    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
116	    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
117	    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
118	    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
119	    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
120	)
121	
122	#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
123	RESERVED_KEYS = (
124	    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
125	    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
126	    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
127	)
128	
129	#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
130	#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
131	#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
132	#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
133	#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
134	#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
135	#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
136	#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
137	#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
138	#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
139	#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
140	#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
141	#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
142	#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
143	#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
144	VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
145	    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
146	    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
147	})
148	
149	
150	def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
151	    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.
152	
153	    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
154	    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:
155	
156	        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.
157	
158	    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
159	    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
160	    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
161	    """
162	    where = f'{path}{k}'
163	    if isinstance(v, str):
164	        vl = v.lower().strip()
165	        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
166	            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
167	            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
168	                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
169	                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
170	                return
171	    elif isinstance(v, dict):
172	        for k2, v2 in v.items():
173	            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
174	                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
175	                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
176	                continue
177	            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
178	                continue
179	            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
180	    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
181	        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
182	            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)
183	
184	
185	@dataclass(frozen=True)
186	class Event:
187	    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
188	    seq: int
189	    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
190	    kind: str
191	    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
192	    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
193	    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
194	
195	    def to_json(self) -> str:
196	        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
197	                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
198	                          sort_keys=True)
199	
200	
201	def _scan_for_conclusions(kind: str, payload: dict) -> list[str]:
202	    """Return the reasons this payload is an ASSERTION rather than an OBSERVATION."""
203	    bad: list[str] = []
204	    for k, v in payload.items():
205	        kl = str(k).lower()
206	        if kl in RESERVED_KEYS:
207	            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
208	            continue
209	        if kl in VERBATIM_KEYS:
210	            continue                       # raw text from the world. It may say anything.
211	        _scan_value(str(k), v, bad)
212	    return bad
213	
214	
215	def _observations(events: list['Event']) -> list['Event']:
216	    """THE OBSERVATIONS ONLY. A reducer NEVER reads another reducer's recorded verdict.
217	
218	    `record_derivation()` writes audit artifacts into the same log, and the module docstring has
219	    always claimed "NO REDUCER READS THEM". That was true only BY ACCIDENT OF KIND CHOICE — nothing
220	    enforced it. It was one line from being false in the worst way: a derived record written with
221	    kind=CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED is picked up by `derive_content_profile`'s `for e in events` loop,
222	    which keeps the LAST match — so the reducer would have read ITS OWN PREVIOUS OUTPUT as if it were
223	    an observation of the bytes, and the label would have become a cache of itself. That is the cookie
224	    banner, again, from the inside.
225	
226	    So the invariant is now structural, in one place, and every derive_*() below goes through it.
227	    """
228	    return [e for e in events if 'derived_by' not in e.payload]
229	
230	
231	class LedgerCorrupt(Exception):
232	    """The persisted log does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never silently repaired."""
233	
234	
235	class Ledger:
236	    """Append-only, DURABLE, and RELOADABLE. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete,
237	    or conclude.
238	
239	    IT DID NOT RELOAD. `__init__` set `self._events = []` and never read the file, so a ledger opened
240	    over an existing JSONL began with an EMPTY HISTORY — while still appending to the end of it. Every
241	    standalone script therefore reduced over the handful of events IT had emitted, not over what
242	    happened. `derive_route_status()` would find one adapter planned instead of four and call the route
243	    COMPLETE; `derive_coverage_status()` would license a scoped absence off a history it could not see.
244	    A reducer over an append-only log that does not read the log is not a reducer over anything — and
245	    it fails in the direction of CONFIDENCE, which is the only direction that ships.
246	    """
247	
248	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None, *, load: bool = True):
249	        self._events: list[Event] = []
250	        self._path = Path(path) if path else None
251	        self._seq = 0
252	        if self._path is not None:
253	            self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
254	            if load and self._path.exists():
255	                self._load()
256	
257	    @classmethod
258	    def load(cls, path: Path | str) -> 'Ledger':
259	        """Open the ledger AS IT STANDS ON DISK. This is the constructor a standalone script wants."""
260	        return cls(path, load=True)
261	
262	    # -- read the history back --------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    def _load(self) -> None:
264	        """A PERSISTED LEDGER IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT, and it is exactly as untrusted as the corpus was.
265	
266	        The emit-time guard is RE-APPLIED here. Bypassing it on read would leave the door it locks
267	        standing open: `echo '{"payload":{"content_status":"FULLTEXT"}}' >> ledger.jsonl` is not a
268	        harder attack than calling emit(), it is an easier one.
269	
270	        A record written by a reducer (it carries `derived_by`) is exempt from that scan, because its
271	        whole job is to hold a derived verdict. Forging one buys nothing: NO REDUCER READS THEM. They
272	        are audit artifacts, and every derive_*() in this file re-derives from the OBSERVATIONS. That
273	        is the difference between a ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped
274	        FULLTEXT on a cookie banner.
275	        """
276	        seen: set[int] = set()
277	        events: list[Event] = []
278	        for i, line in enumerate(self._path.read_text().splitlines(), 1):
279	            line = line.strip()
280	            if not line:
281	                continue
282	            where = f'{self._path.name}:{i}'
283	            try:
284	                d = json.loads(line)
285	            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
286	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: not JSON — {e}')
287	            missing = {'seq', 'unit', 'kind', 'actor', 'payload', 'ts'} - set(d)
288	            if missing:
289	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
290	            if d['kind'] not in EventKind.ALL:
291	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: unknown event kind {d["kind"]!r}')
292	            if not isinstance(d['payload'], dict):
293	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: payload is not an object')
294	            if not isinstance(d['seq'], int) or d['seq'] in seen:
295	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: seq {d["seq"]!r} is duplicated or not an integer — an '
296	                                    f'append-only log with a repeated sequence number has been edited')
297	            if 'derived_by' not in d['payload']:
298	                problems = _scan_for_conclusions(d['kind'], d['payload'])
299	                if problems:
300	                    raise LedgerCorrupt(
301	                        f'{where}: this line writes a CONCLUSION into the ledger — the emit guard '
302	                        f'refuses it, and so does the loader:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
303	            seen.add(d['seq'])
304	            events.append(Event(seq=d['seq'], unit=d['unit'], kind=d['kind'], actor=d['actor'],
305	                                payload=d['payload'], ts=d['ts']))
306	        events.sort(key=lambda e: e.seq)
307	        self._events = events
308	        self._seq = max(seen, default=0)     # ...so the next append CONTINUES the log, never collides
309	
310	    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
311	    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
312	        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
313	            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
314	        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
315	        if problems:
316	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
317	                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
318	        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)
319	
320	    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
321	        self._seq += 1
322	        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
323	        self._events.append(ev)
324	        if self._path:
325	            # One line, one open, closed immediately: the record is with the OS before emit() returns.
326	            # An event that is in memory but not on disk is a history that disagrees with itself the
327	            # moment the process dies.
328	            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
329	                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
330	                fh.flush()
331	        return ev
332	
333	    # -- the reducer's audit trail --------------------------------------------------------------
334	    def record_derivation(self, unit: str, kind: str, reducer: str, inputs: list[int],
335	                          **verdict) -> Event:
336	        """A reducer may append its own verdict — FOR AUDIT ONLY. Nobody else can call this.
337	
338	        The frame check below is not decoration. `emit()` is the door components use, and it refuses
339	        conclusions. This is the door the reducer uses, and it is bolted from the inside: if the
340	        calling frame is not this module, it raises. There is no third door.
341	
342	        Downstream reducers NEVER TRUST these records — they RE-DERIVE from the observations. A
343	        recorded verdict is an audit artifact, not an authority. That is the difference between a
344	        ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped "FULLTEXT" on a cookie banner.
345	        """
346	        import inspect
347	        caller = inspect.currentframe().f_back
348	        if caller.f_globals.get('__name__') != __name__:
349	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
350	                f'{caller.f_globals.get("__name__")!r} tried to record a DERIVED label directly. '
351	                f'Only a reducer inside {__name__} may. Emit OBSERVATIONS and call derive_*().')
352	        return self._append(unit, kind, reducer,
353	                            {**verdict, 'derived_by': reducer, 'from_events': list(inputs)})
354	
355	    # -- read side ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
356	    def events(self, unit: str | None = None, kind: str | None = None) -> list[Event]:
357	        return [e for e in self._events
358	                if (unit is None or e.unit == unit) and (kind is None or e.kind == kind)]
359	
360	    def units(self) -> list[str]:
361	        return list(dict.fromkeys(e.unit for e in self._events))
362	
363	    def __len__(self) -> int:
364	        return len(self._events)
365	
366	
367	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
368	# OBSERVERS — pure functions from raw content to OBSERVATIONS. They conclude nothing.
369	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
370	
371	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
372	_CHROME = re.compile(
373	    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
374	    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
375	    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
376	    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
377	    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
378	    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
379	    re.I)
380	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
381	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
382	    r'nber working paper (?:series|no)|national bureau of economic research|'
383	    r'iza (?:dp|discussion paper)|this (?:draft|version):|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|'
384	    r'do not (?:cite|quote) without|arxiv:\d', re.I)
385	
386	
387	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
388	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
389	
390	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
391	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
392	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
393	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
394	    """
395	    words = text.split()
396	    header = text[:header_chars]
397	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
398	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
399	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
400	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
401	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
402	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
403	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
404	    return {
405	        'word_count': len(words),
406	        'char_count': len(text),
407	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
408	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
409	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
410	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
411	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
412	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
413	        'header_real_words': header_real,
414	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
415	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
416	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
417	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
418	        'identity_window': header,
419	        'header_text': header[:400],
420	    }
421	
422	
423	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
424	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
425	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
426	
427	# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------
428	
429	RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
430	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
431	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
432	ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
433	NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
434	
435	
436	#: The four kinds that constitute A REQUEST. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED and MANIFESTATION_FETCHED also carry
437	#: an `adapter` (for provenance — WHICH backend found this), but they are not request outcomes and must
438	#: not be read as one.
439	_REQUEST_KINDS = (EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
440	                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)
441	
442	#: Worst-first. An adapter that hung on one request and answered another has NOT answered: we asked it
443	#: something and never found out. Between RESPONDED and NOT_INDEXED, RESPONDED wins — a backend that
444	#: answered "not in my index" for one query and returned a paper for another IS WORKING.
445	_OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE = (NO_OUTCOME, BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED, RESPONDED, NOT_INDEXED)
446	
447	
448	def _outcome_of_request(evs: list[Event]) -> str:
449	    """The outcome of ONE logical request, read from its FINAL terminal event.
450	
451	    A retry loop emits BACKEND_ATTEMPTED + one terminal event PER ATTEMPT, all sharing a `request_id`.
452	    So a request reads:  ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED(200)
453	
454	    The 429s ARE NOT DELETED — they are on disk forever, and `Ledger` has no operation that could
455	    remove them. But a throttle the backoff RESOLVED and a throttle we GAVE UP ON are different facts
456	    about the world, and only the second one means we never got the answer. Reading "any 429 anywhere"
457	    as BACKEND_FAILED would mark every route degraded the moment a single retry succeeded, and a route
458	    that is always degraded can never license a scoped absence — which sounds conservative and is
459	    actually just as blind as always calling it complete.
460	
461	        exhausted retries -> final terminal event is THROTTLED         -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
462	        429 then 200      -> final terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED.
463	    """
464	    terminal = [e for e in evs if e.kind in (EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, EventKind.THROTTLED,
465	                                             EventKind.BLOCKED)]
466	    if not terminal:
467	        return NO_OUTCOME               # attempted and never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
468	    e = terminal[-1]
469	    if e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED:
470	        return BACKEND_FAILED
471	    if e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED:
472	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
473	    code = e.payload.get('http_status')
474	    if code in (429, 503):
475	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # even if someone logged it as a "response"
476	    if code in (401, 403):
477	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
478	    if code == 404:
479	        return NOT_INDEXED
480	    if e.payload.get('transport_error'):
481	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # timeout / DNS / reset / bad JSON / 5xx
482	    return RESPONDED
483	
484	
485	def derive_backend_outcome(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> str:
486	    """What actually happened to one adapter. `None` is not an answer — it is four different worlds.
487	
488	    An adapter may be asked more than once (four candidate PDFs from one content host; a DOI lookup and
489	    a title search). Each logical request is scored on its own; the adapter reports its WORST.
490	    """
491	    mine = [e for e in _observations(events)
492	            if e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter and e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS]
493	    if not mine:
494	        return NO_OUTCOME
495	
496	    # Events carrying no `request_id` are a HISTORY WRITTEN BEFORE RETRIES EXISTED (our replay, the
497	    # hand-written invariants, any older log). They are one bucket, scored by the original rule: ANY
498	    # throttle anywhere condemns the adapter. Back-compat is not politeness here — a reducer that
499	    # changed its answer about a log it had already reduced would make the whole record unreadable.
500	    buckets: dict[str, list[Event]] = {}
501	    for e in mine:
502	        buckets.setdefault(str(e.payload.get('request_id') or ''), []).append(e)
503	
504	    outcomes: list[str] = []
505	    for rid, evs in buckets.items():
506	        if rid == '':
507	            if any(e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED for e in evs):
508	                outcomes.append(BACKEND_FAILED)
509	            elif any(e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED for e in evs):
510	                outcomes.append(ACCESS_BLOCKED)
511	            else:
512	                first = [e for e in evs if e.kind == EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED][:1]
513	                outcomes.append(_outcome_of_request(first) if first else NO_OUTCOME)
514	        else:
515	            outcomes.append(_outcome_of_request(evs))
516	
517	    for o in _OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE:
518	        if o in outcomes:
519	            return o
520	    return NO_OUTCOME
521	
522	
523	# ---- 2. route status -------------------------------------------------------------------------
524	
525	@dataclass(frozen=True)
526	class RouteStatus:
527	    state: str                     # UNROUTED | INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE_DEGRADED | COMPLETE
528	    planned: tuple[str, ...]
529	    outcomes: dict[str, str]
530	    missing: tuple[str, ...]       # planned adapters with NO terminal outcome
531	    failed: tuple[str, ...]        # adapters that were throttled/blocked/errored
532	    budget_stopped: bool
533	
534	    @property
535	    def supports_absence(self) -> bool:
536	        """THE question. Only an ADEQUATE route licenses "we looked, and it is not there."
537	
538	        Every planned adapter must have genuinely ANSWERED. One 429, one hang, or a budget stop and
539	        the honest label is SEARCH_FAILED — we do not know, and we may not say we do.
540	        """
541	        return (self.state == 'COMPLETE'
542	                and not self.missing and not self.failed and not self.budget_stopped
543	                and bool(self.planned))
544	
545	
546	def derive_route_status(events: list[Event]) -> RouteStatus:
547	    """route_complete means EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
548	
549	    It NEVER means "an adapter was mapped".
550	    """
551	    events = _observations(events)
552	    planned: list[str] = []
553	    for e in events:
554	        if e.kind == EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED:
555	            planned += [a for a in e.payload.get('adapters', []) if a not in planned]
556	    if not planned:
557	        return RouteStatus('UNROUTED', (), {}, (), (), False)
558	
559	    budget = any(e.kind == EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED for e in events)
560	    outcomes = {a: derive_backend_outcome(events, a) for a in planned}
561	    missing = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o == NO_OUTCOME)
562	
563	    # ── A BLOCKED RETRIEVAL IS A FAILED ROUTE. ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
564	    # The SEARCH adapters are the ones in ROUTE_PLANNED. But finding the document's address is not
565	    # holding the document: after a search answers, we go to the CONTENT HOST for the bytes, and that
566	    # host is a backend too — it can throttle us, and it can shut the door in our face.
567	    #
568	    # This was live, and it was the whole disease wearing a new coat. Autor (2015), JEP:
569	    #
570	    #     crossref                RESPONDED        (the metadata)
571	    #     unpaywall               RESPONDED        (...and here is where the PDF lives)
572	    #     content:www.aeaweb.org  ACCESS_BLOCKED   HTTP 403
573	    #
574	    # and because the content host was not a PLANNED adapter, the route read COMPLETE, `supports_
575	    # absence` read TRUE, and coverage reduced to SEARCHED_NONE — "a SCOPED ABSENCE, and the only
576	    # state that licenses one". A 403 FROM THE PUBLISHER had become a statement that the literature
577	    # is silent. That is a fact about ENTITLEMENT, printed as a fact about the world: the identical
578	    # error to reading a 429 as "no free copy exists", committed one layer down, by the cure.
579	    #
580	    # So EVERY adapter that failed counts against the route — planned or not. We do not get to say we
581	    # looked and found nothing when we were shown the door.
582	    all_adapters = {e.payload.get('adapter') for e in events
583	                    if e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS and e.payload.get('adapter')}
584	    for a in sorted(all_adapters - set(planned)):
585	        o = derive_backend_outcome(events, a)
586	        if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED):
587	            outcomes[a] = o
588	    failed = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED))
589	
590	    if missing:
591	        state = 'INCOMPLETE'
592	    elif failed or budget:
593	        state = 'COMPLETE_DEGRADED'     # every adapter has a record — but the search DID NOT WORK
594	    else:
595	        state = 'COMPLETE'
596	    return RouteStatus(state, tuple(planned), outcomes, missing, failed, budget)
597	
598	
599	# ---- 3. content profile — DRIVEN BY THE ONE REGISTRY, NOT BY A NUMBER ------------------------
600	#
601	# THERE WAS A `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` HERE. It is deleted, and this comment is its headstone.
602	#
603	# It was written in the fix for the bug it is. `provenance.py`'s own header says, in Sol's words: "A
604	# universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
605	# trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles" — and then this
606	# module, the CURE, reinstated one universal number two files away, where nothing compared them. It
607	# did not merely contradict the principle; it contradicted `provenance.profile()`, whose scholarly
608	# floor is 1,200. TWO REDUCERS THAT DISAGREE ARE NOT A SHARED RULE. Which document a card could cite
609	# depended on which module happened to look at it last.
610	#
611	# So completeness is not decided here at all. It is READ FROM THE REGISTRY, by the ONE reducer:
612	#
613	#     provenance.KIND_PROFILE      what each artifact kind is, and its stub floor (or None)
614	#     provenance.judge_completeness(kind, n_words, extraction_verdict)
615	#
616	# A judicial opinion, a statute section and a trial-registry record have stub_floor=None and are
617	# COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. A cookie banner is incomplete at any length. Neither fact is a word count.
618	
619	C_FULLTEXT     = 'FULLTEXT'
620	C_ABSTRACT     = 'ABSTRACT'
621	C_CITATION     = 'CITATION_ONLY'
622	C_NOT_DOC      = 'NOT_A_DOCUMENT'        # a cookie banner, a login wall, a paywall interstitial
623	C_UNREADABLE   = 'UNREADABLE_ENCODING'   # a PDF whose glyphs never decoded
624	
625	GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX = 0.10   # > this share of (cid:NN) tokens => the font never decoded
626	CHROME_PER_1K_MAX = 5.0    # web furniture DENSITY. Density, not length — see below.
627	
628	#: THE LEGACY THREE-WAY VOCABULARY. `cellcog_composer` and `evidence_miner` both select their usable
629	#: corpus with `content_status != 'CITATION_ONLY'`, so the coarse label they contract on is a
630	#: PROJECTION of the five-way derived class — computed in ONE place, here, and never by a word count.
631	#:
632	#: NOTE THE DIRECTION OF THE PROJECTION. NOT_A_DOCUMENT and UNREADABLE_ENCODING collapse to
633	#: CITATION_ONLY — that is, TO UNUSABLE. It is tempting to send them to ABSTRACT_ONLY (they have
634	#: hundreds of words, after all, and a word-count rule sends them there automatically — which is what
635	#: `corpus_truth.FULLTEXT_MIN=2500` did). That would make a COOKIE BANNER MINABLE. The 535-word aeaweb
636	#: banner and the 548-word ORA landing page would both have passed `!= 'CITATION_ONLY'` and been mined
637	#: as the paper's own summary. Every collapse in this table must fail SAFE, and the safe direction for
638	#: bytes that are not a document is: not evidence.
639	LEGACY_STATUS = {
640	    C_FULLTEXT:   'FULLTEXT',
641	    C_ABSTRACT:   'ABSTRACT_ONLY',
642	    C_CITATION:   'CITATION_ONLY',
643	    C_NOT_DOC:    'CITATION_ONLY',      # a cookie banner is NOT an abstract of the paper
644	    C_UNREADABLE: 'CITATION_ONLY',      # a PDF whose font never decoded is not a summary of anything
645	}
646	
647	#: The content class each artifact kind reports as. The finding-bearing kinds are absent on purpose:
648	#: for those, THE REGISTRY decides (complete => FULLTEXT), and hardcoding them here would be a second
649	#: opinion about completeness, which is the whole defect.
650	_CLASS_OF_KIND = {
651	    'extraction_failure': C_UNREADABLE,
652	    'landing_page':       C_NOT_DOC,
653	    'wrong_work':         C_NOT_DOC,
654	    'abstract':           C_ABSTRACT,
655	    'citation_only':      C_CITATION,
656	    'unknown':            C_CITATION,
657	}
658	
659	
660	def derive_artifact_kind(prof: dict, source_type: str = '') -> tuple[str, str]:
661	    """WHAT KIND OF DOCUMENT IS THIS? Registry-driven, from OBSERVATIONS only.
662	
663	    `source_type` is the kind of RECORD the unit came from — a Crossref `journal-article`, a court's
664	    opinions endpoint, ClinicalTrials.gov. It selects WHICH COMPLETENESS PROFILE APPLIES and nothing
665	    else, and it is REBUTTABLE BY THE BYTES: the demotions below run first, so a login wall served by
666	    a court's website is a login wall.
667	
668	    It is NOT `fulltext_source` wearing a new hat. `fulltext_source='working_paper'` was a claim about
669	    WHAT THE DOCUMENT IS, written by whichever script ran, believed by everyone, and false in both
670	    directions. `source_type` cannot promote anything: it cannot make a cookie banner an article, and
671	    it cannot make a working paper a journal version — the header stamp decides that, in
672	    derive_semantic_binding(), untouched. All it can do is stop us judging a judicial opinion by a
673	    journal article's word count.
674	    """
675	    wc = prof.get('word_count', 0)
676	    readable = prof.get('readable_word_count', 0)
677	    chrome = prof.get('chrome_markers', 0)
678	    glyph = prof.get('glyph_garbage_ratio', 0)
679	
680	    # Which profile do we judge this against? Default: the scholarly one (every row in this corpus is
681	    # a Crossref journal-article). An UNREGISTERED type is not silently accepted — it falls back to
682	    # scholarly and SAYS SO, because a kind nobody registered has no completeness profile to use.
683	    work_kind, _claimed = _SOURCE_TYPE.get((source_type or '').strip().lower(), ('study', ''))
684	    declared = _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind) or 'journal_article'
685	    spec = KIND_PROFILE[declared]
686	    floor = spec['stub_floor']
687	    src = f'source_type={source_type!r}' if source_type else 'no source_type declared (scholarly default)'
688	
689	    if wc <= 0:
690	        return 'citation_only', 'we hold no bytes at all'
691	    if glyph > GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX:
692	        return 'extraction_failure', (f'{glyph:.0%} of tokens are (cid:NN) glyph codes — the font '
693	                                      f'never decoded')
694	    # A cookie banner is not a short paper. It is NOT A PAPER — and that is true of a 300-word statute
695	    # section's landing page too, which is why the LENGTH half of this test applies only where there
696	    # IS a length below which a document is a fragment. Where there is not (stub_floor=None), only
697	    # web-furniture DENSITY may condemn it.
698	    chrome_per_kw = 1000.0 * chrome / max(1, wc)
699	    if chrome and (chrome_per_kw >= CHROME_PER_1K_MAX or (floor is not None and wc < floor)):
700	        return 'landing_page', (f'{chrome} site-chrome marker(s) in {wc}w ({chrome_per_kw:.0f}/1k) — '
701	                                f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
702	    if floor is not None and readable < floor:
703	        # A SCHOLARLY FRAGMENT. Which fragment it is, is the registry's abstract_floor — not a second
704	        # universal number invented here.
705	        af = spec['abstract_floor']
706	        if af is not None and readable < af:
707	            return 'citation_only', f'{readable:,} readable words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
708	        return 'abstract', (f'{readable:,} readable words is below the stub floor for `{declared}` '
709	                            f'({floor:,}) — a fragment of the article, not the article')
710	    return declared, f'{readable:,} readable words; judged as `{declared}` ({src})'
711	
712	
713	def _source_type_of(events: list[Event]) -> str:
714	    """The record type OBSERVED for this unit. An observation about what we asked for, with a basis."""
715	    for e in reversed(_observations(events)):
716	        st = e.payload.get('source_type')
717	        if st:
718	            return str(st)
719	    return ''
720	
721	
722	#: WHICH DOCUMENT WE HOLD IS BETTER. Used ONLY to pick which of several manifestations a unit-level
723	#: question is about — never to decide what any one of them IS.
724	_CLASS_RANK = {C_FULLTEXT: 4, C_ABSTRACT: 3, C_CITATION: 2, C_NOT_DOC: 1, C_UNREADABLE: 1}
725	
726	
727	def _holdings(events: list[Event]) -> list[tuple[dict, dict]]:
728	    """Every (manifestation, ITS OWN profile) PAIR this unit holds, in order.
729	
730	    `record_manifestation()` emits MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED adjacently, so a
731	    pair is a document and the profile OF THAT DOCUMENT. Pairing matters: without it, a unit holding a
732	    body and an abstract would answer identity questions from one and completeness questions from the
733	    other, and the answer would describe no document that exists.
734	    """
735	    pairs: list[tuple[dict, dict]] = []
736	    pending: dict | None = None
737	    for e in _observations(events):
738	        if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED:
739	            pending = e.payload
740	        elif e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED:
741	            pairs.append((pending or {}, e.payload))
742	            pending = None
743	    return pairs
744	
745	
746	def _classify(prof: dict, source_type: str) -> tuple[str, dict]:
747	    """ONE document's bytes -> its content class. The whole of the completeness rule, for one blob."""
748	    kind, basis = derive_artifact_kind(prof, source_type)
749	    readable = prof.get('readable_word_count', 0) or prof.get('word_count', 0)
750	    verdict = 'CORRUPT' if prof.get('glyph_garbage_ratio', 0) > GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX else 'CLEAN'
751	    # THE ONE REDUCER. Not a number in this file.
752	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, readable, verdict)
753	    cls = _CLASS_OF_KIND.get(kind)
754	    if cls is None:                       # a finding-bearing kind: THE REGISTRY says if it is whole
755	        cls = C_FULLTEXT if complete else C_ABSTRACT
756	    info = {'reason': basis if complete else '; '.join(reasons) or basis,
757	            'artifact_kind': kind, 'complete': complete, **prof}
758	    return cls, info
759	
760	
761	def _best_holding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[dict, dict, str, dict] | None:
762	    """THE BEST DOCUMENT WE HOLD for this unit -> (manifestation, profile, class, info).
763	
764	    IT USED TO TAKE THE LAST ONE PROFILED, AND THAT WAS A LIVE DEFECT — introduced, of course, in the
765	    fix for the defect it is. Once acquisition began retaining EVERY set of bytes instead of deleting
766	    all but one, a work commonly held two: the fetched body AND the bibliography's abstract. The
767	    abstract was ingested second, so `for e in events: prof = e.payload` ended holding the ABSTRACT'S
768	    profile — and the unit's label was derived from it.
769	
770	    Result, on the real corpus, before this was fixed:
771	
772	        Damioli (2021)   label = CITATION_ONLY    while the row carried 13,085 words
773	        Tolan (2021)     label = ABSTRACT         while the row carried 18,225 words
774	
775	    A label that asserts less than its content supports is the same disease as one that asserts more:
776	    it is a claim about a document nobody is holding. Five rows had it, and every one of them would
777	    have been skipped by the miner.
778	
779	    So the question "what do we hold for this work?" is answered by THE BEST DOCUMENT WE HOLD. A cookie
780	    banner sitting beside a journal article does not make the article an abstract; we hold the article.
781	    """
782	    pairs = _holdings(events)
783	    if not pairs:
784	        return None
785	    st = _source_type_of(events)
786	    scored = []
787	    for manif, prof in pairs:
788	        cls, info = _classify(prof, st)
789	        scored.append((_CLASS_RANK.get(cls, 0), prof.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0,
790	                       manif, prof, cls, info))
791	    best = max(scored, key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]))
792	    return best[2], best[3], best[4], best[5]
793	
794	
795	def derive_content_profile(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
796	    """FULLTEXT IS EARNED — AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR ITS KIND. It is never a thing a fetcher declares,
797	    and it is never a number this module keeps to itself."""
798	    best = _best_holding(events)
799	    if best is None:
800	        return C_CITATION, {'reason': 'no content was ever profiled'}
801	    _manif, _prof, cls, info = best
802	    n = len(_holdings(events))
803	    if n > 1:
804	        info = {**info, 'n_documents_held': n,
805	                'reason': f'{info["reason"]} (the best of {n} documents held for this work)'}
806	    return cls, info
807	
808	
809	# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------
810	
811	SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
812	VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
813	VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
814	DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
815	UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
816	
817	
818	def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
819	    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
820	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}
821	
822	
823	def derive_semantic_binding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
824	    """Is the text we fetched the work we asked for? A fetcher MAY NOT ANSWER THIS ABOUT ITSELF.
825	
826	    Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
827	    DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven."
828	    """
829	    # THE SAME DOCUMENT THE LABEL DESCRIBES. `_best_holding` returns a manifestation TOGETHER WITH ITS
830	    # OWN profile — so identity is checked against the header of the very bytes whose completeness was
831	    # judged. Reading "the last manifestation" and "the last profile" independently could pair one
832	    # document's byline with another document's word count, and answer about a document that does not
833	    # exist.
834	    best = _best_holding(events)
835	    if best is None:
836	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': 'nothing was fetched'}
837	    fetched, prof, _cls, _info = best
838	
839	    # The identity window is BOUNDED (see observe_text): a whole-document scan finds every author in
840	    # the references and "confirms" anything you ask it to.
841	    window = fetched.get('document_title', '') or prof.get('identity_window', '') \
842	        or prof.get('header_text', '')
843	    want_t = _norm(fetched.get('requested_title', ''))
844	    got_t = _norm(window)
845	    want_a = {a.lower() for a in fetched.get('requested_authors', [])}
846	    byline = (fetched.get('byline', '') or window).lower()
847	
848	    overlap = len(want_t & got_t) / max(1, len(want_t))
849	    author_hit = any(a in byline for a in want_a if a)
850	    title_match = overlap >= 0.90
851	    # A SPECIFIC title (>=4 content words) matching in full is itself strong identity evidence.
852	    # A GENERIC one is not: {rise, machines} sits entirely inside {mathematics, rise, machines}.
853	    title_specific = len(want_t) >= 4
854	
855	    ev = {'title_overlap': round(overlap, 2), 'author_in_byline': author_hit,
856	          'title_content_words': len(want_t),
857	          'requested_title': fetched.get('requested_title', '')[:70],
858	          'header_text': (prof.get('header_text') or '')[:120]}
859	
860	    # ── AN UNREADABLE HEADER IS NOT A STRANGER'S PAPER. ───────────────────────────────────────
861	    # Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) arrives as a PDF whose title page is pure (cid:NN) glyph codes:
862	    # ZERO readable words in the header, though the BODY has 10,902. An earlier version of this
863	    # reducer read "no title, no author" as DIFFERENT_WORK — turning a FONT-ENCODING FAILURE into a
864	    # confident claim about whose paper it is. That is this project's whole disease, committed by
865	    # the cure. We cannot read it, therefore we cannot bind it. Say exactly that.
866	    if prof.get('header_real_words', 0) < 10:
867	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'the header decoded to {prof.get("header_real_words", 0)} '
868	                                      f'readable words — we CANNOT READ it, so we cannot establish '
869	                                      f'identity. Not a stranger\'s paper: an unreadable one', **ev}
870	
871	    # ── IDENTITY IS CONFIRMED BY AN AUTHOR, OR BY A SPECIFIC TITLE IN FULL. ───────────────────
872	    confirmed = author_hit or (title_match and title_specific)
873	
874	    if not confirmed:
875	        # DIFFERENT_WORK is a STRONG CLAIM ("we are holding a stranger's paper") and it needs
876	        # POSITIVE evidence. Absence of a confirming author is NOT that evidence — it is absence of
877	        # evidence, and reading the two as the same is how "429" became "no free copy exists".
878	        if title_match and not title_specific:
879	            return DIFFERENT_WORK, {

881	                          f'requested author appears in the header. A {overlap:.0%} match on a '
882	                          f'GENERIC title is a TITLE COLLISION, not identity — the short title sits '
883	                          f'inside a stranger\'s longer one', **ev}
884	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no requested author in the '
885	                                      f'header — identity is NOT established, so this text may not '
886	                                      f'be attributed to this source', **ev}
887	
888	    # ...an author alone is not identity either: prolific authors write many papers.
889	    if author_hit and not title_match and not title_specific:
890	        return DIFFERENT_WORK, {'reason': f'the author matches but the title does not ({overlap:.0%}) '
891	                                          f'— same author, DIFFERENT PAPER', **ev}
892	
893	    if prof.get('published_stamp_in_header'):
894	        return VERSION_PUBLISHED, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, and the header carries a '
895	                                             'published-version/refereed stamp', **ev}
896	    if prof.get('preprint_stamp_in_header'):
897	        return VERSION_PREPRINT, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, but the header carries a '
898	                                            'preprint/working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES '
899	                                            'NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version '
900	                                            'equivalence is proven', **ev}

# LLM inference path + report-data jurisdiction, NOT search queries (which
# carry no confidential content). Serper (US) is disclosed in /transparency.
#
# scripts/egress_lockdown.sh resolves each domain to A AND AAAA records and
# installs iptables (IPv4) + ip6tables (IPv6) rules in OUTPUT (host) +
# DOCKER-USER (container). Everything else 80/443 is DROP-ed on BOTH stacks.
#
# Run lockdown AFTER first `docker compose build` so build-time hosts
# (deb.debian.org, pypi.org, files.pythonhosted.org, registry.npmjs.org)
# can be removed from the runtime allowlist if desired.

# ----- LLM (transition: OpenRouter US; production: private vLLM on OVH H200) -----
# When POLARIS_LLM_BACKEND=vllm, no external LLM hosts hit at all (private
# network to OVH). When POLARIS_LLM_BACKEND=openrouter (transition), the
# 2 lines below are required:
openrouter.ai
api.openrouter.ai

# ----- Live retrieval (Serper US — disclosed; user-accepted I-carney-010) -----
# Serper is US-based. Per user directive 2026-05-13 it stays as the search
# backend: search queries carry no confidential content, and the generated
# reports stay on sovereign infrastructure. Disclosed plainly in
# /transparency §4. The sovereignty constraint is the LLM inference path +
# report-data jurisdiction, not the search-query egress.
google.serper.dev
# Semantic Scholar (US AI2 non-profit) — disclosed in /transparency,
# kept for the academic-bib path; remove for stricter sovereignty.
api.semanticscholar.org

# ----- T1 corpus (regulatory + clinical guideline) — sovereign egress -----
# Per src/polaris_graph/retrieval/{evidence_selector,tier_classifier,
# domain_backends,frame_fetcher}.py these are the cleared T1 hosts. Audit-
# grade demo material is ONLY ever T1; T2/T3 publisher arbitrary URLs are
# dropped by sovereignty filter AND by this egress (defense in depth).
#
# CRITICAL: iptables resolves each hostname to ONE set of A/AAAA records;
# bare-domain vs www-prefixed vs sub-prefixed CDN edges resolve to DIFFERENT
# IPs. egress_lockdown.sh per Codex iter-2 P1-3 must allowlist every
# hostname pipeline-A can dispatch httpx.Client() against. Iter-2 listed
# only the bare-domain forms; iter-3 adds the www. + sub-prefixed forms
# the code can produce.

# US FDA (govt, not US company)
fda.gov
www.fda.gov
accessdata.fda.gov
nctr-crs.fda.gov
labels.fda.gov
# US ClinicalTrials.gov
clinicaltrials.gov
www.clinicaltrials.gov
# US CDC + NASA (tier_classifier REGULATORY_DOMAINS)
cdc.gov
www.cdc.gov
# US NCBI / NLM literature aggregator (sovereignty-disclosed; T1 corpus path)
ncbi.nlm.nih.gov
